results.title: Real-time Power System Simulation with Hardware Devices through DNP3 in Cyber-Physical Testbed
results.citation: Hao Huang et al. (2021) Real-time Power System Simulation with Hardware Devices through DNP3 in Cyber-Physical Testbed doi:10.1109/TPEC51183.2021.9384947
results.abstract: Modern power grids are dependent on communication systems for data collection, visualization, and control. Distributed Network Protocol 3 (DNP3) is commonly used in supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems in power systems to allow control system software and hardware to communicate. To study the dependencies between communication network security, power system data collection, and industrial hardware, it is important to enable communication capabilities with real-time power system simulation. In this paper, we present the integration of new functionality of a power systems dynamic simulation package into our cyber-physical power system testbed that supports real-time power system data transfer using DNP3, demonstrated with an industrial real-time automation controller (RTAC). The usage and configuration of DNP3 with real-world equipment in to achieve power system monitoring and control of a large-scale synthetic electric grid via this DNP3 communication is presented. Then, an exemplar of DNP3 data collection and control is achieved in software and hardware using the 2000-bus Texas synthetic grid.

results.title: Mixed-Integer Optimization for Bio-Inspired Robust Power Network Design
results.citation: Hao Huang et al. (2020) Mixed-Integer Optimization for Bio-Inspired Robust Power Network Design doi:10.1109/NAPS50074.2021.9449755
results.abstract: Power systems are susceptible to natural threats including hurricanes and floods. Modern power grids are also increasingly threatened by cyber attacks. Existing approaches that help improve power system security and resilience may not be sufficient; this is evidenced by the continued challenge to supply energy to all customers during severe events. This paper presents an approach to address this challenge through bio-inspired power system network design to improve system reliability and resilience against disturbances. Inspired by naturally robust ecosystems, this paper considers the optimal ecological robustness that recognizes a unique balance between pathway efficiency and redundancy to ensure the survivability against disruptive events for given networks. This paper presents an approach that maximizes ecological robustness in transmission network design by formulating a mixed-integer nonlinear programming optimization problem with power system constraints. The results show the increase of the optimized power system's robustness and the improved reliability with less violations under N-x contingencies.

results.title: Dynamic Exploitation Gaussian Bare-Bones Bat Algorithm for Optimal Reactive Power Dispatch to Improve the Safety and Stability of Power System
results.citation: Zhaoyang Qu et al. (2021) Dynamic Exploitation Gaussian Bare-Bones Bat Algorithm for Optimal Reactive Power Dispatch to Improve the Safety and Stability of Power System doi:10.1049/rpg2.12428
results.abstract: In this paper, a novel Gaussian bare-bones bat algorithm (GBBBA) and its modified version named as dynamic exploitation Gaussian bare-bones bat algorithm (DeGBBBA) are proposed for solving optimal reactive power dispatch (ORPD) problem. The optimal reactive power dispatch (ORPD) plays a fundamental role in ensuring stable, secure, reliable as well as economical operation of the power system. The ORPD problem is formulated as a complex and nonlinear optimization problem of mixed integers including both discrete and continuous control variables. Bat algorithm (BA) is one of the most popular metaheuristic algorithms which mimics the echolocation of the microbats and which has also outperformed some other metaheuristic algorithms in solving various optimization problems. Nevertheless, the standard BA may fail to balance exploration and exploitation for some optimization problems and hence it may often fall into local optima. The proposed GBBBA employs the Gaussian distribution in updating the bat positions in an effort to mitigate the premature convergence problem associated with the standard BA. The GBBBA takes advantages of Gaussian sampling which begins from exploration and continues to exploitation. DeGBBBA is an advanced variant of GBBBA in which a modified Gaussian distribution is introduced so as to allow the dynamic adaptation of exploitation and exploitation in the proposed algorithm. Both GBBBA and DeGBBBA are used to determine the optimal settings of generator bus voltages, tap setting transformers and shunt reactive sources in order to minimize the active power loss, total voltage deviations and voltage stability index. Simulation results show that GBBBA and DeGBBBA are robust and effective in solving the ORPD problem.

results.title: hynet: An Optimal Power Flow Framework for Hybrid AC/DC Power Systems
results.citation: Matthias Hotz et al. (2018) hynet: An Optimal Power Flow Framework for Hybrid AC/DC Power Systems doi:10.1109/TPWRS.2019.2942988
results.abstract: High-voltage direct current (HVDC) systems are increasingly incorporated into today's AC power grids, necessitating optimal power flow (OPF) tools for the analysis, planning, and operation of such hybrid systems. To this end, we introduce hynet, a Python-based open-source OPF framework for hybrid AC/DC grids with point-to-point and radial multi-terminal HVDC systems. hynet's design promotes ease of use and extensibility, which is supported by the particular mathematical model and software design presented in this paper. The system model features a unified representation of AC and DC subgrids as well as a concise and flexible converter model, which enable the compact description of a hybrid AC/DC power system and its OPF problem. To support convex relaxation based OPF solution techniques, a state space relaxation is introduced to obtain a unified OPF formulation that is analogous to the OPF of AC power systems. This enables the direct generalization of relaxation-related results for AC grids to hybrid AC/DC grids, which is shown for the semidefinite and second-order cone relaxation as well as associated results on exactness and locational marginal prices. Finally, hynet's object-oriented software design is discussed, which provides extensibility via inheritance and standard design patterns, and its robust and competitive performance is illustrated with case studies.

results.title: Stability Analysis in Large-scale Centralized Bidirectional Inverter-based Stations Connected to Bulk Power Systems through AC and DC Connections
results.citation: Qiang Fu et al. (2026) Stability Analysis in Large-scale Centralized Bidirectional Inverter-based Stations Connected to Bulk Power Systems through AC and DC Connections arXiv:2606.17565v1
results.abstract: Massive controlled DC resources (CDCRs), such as battery energy storage systems, are connected to AC power systems through bidirectional inverters for power balance requirements. This study investigates converter-driven stability (CDS) issues in the sub-synchronous frequency range caused by large-scale bidirectional inverter-based stations (IBSs). The impacts of the AC and DC connections of IBSs on subsynchronous oscillations (SSOs) are compared by examining three factors: the number of CDCRs, power flow direction, and control parameters of the inverters. For AC connections, IBSs may induce instability as the number of CDCRs increases, regardless of the power flow direction. To maintain stability, the maximum power amplitude of the IBS is calculated. It is found that switching to DC connections can reduce these instability risks if the DC line resistance is much less than the AC line reactance. Moreover, the method of tuning control parameters is demonstrated to be more effective in improving power-related critical stability under DC connections. Therefore, The DC-IBS is preferred for high-voltage transmission. Finally, the conclusions are validated in power systems connected with both AC- and DC-IBSs under various network topologies and system scales.

results.title: Cascading Power Outages Propagate Locally in an Influence Graph that is not the Actual Grid Topology
results.citation: Paul D. H. Hines et al. (2015) Cascading Power Outages Propagate Locally in an Influence Graph that is not the Actual Grid Topology doi:10.1109/TPWRS.2016.2578259
results.abstract: In a cascading power transmission outage, component outages propagate non-locally, after one component outages, the next failure may be very distant, both topologically and geographically. As a result, simple models of topological contagion do not accurately represent the propagation of cascades in power systems. However, cascading power outages do follow patterns, some of which are useful in understanding and reducing blackout risk. This paper describes a method by which the data from many cascading failure simulations can be transformed into a graph-based model of influences that provides actionable information about the many ways that cascades propagate in a particular system. The resulting "influence graph" model is Markovian, in that component outage probabilities depend only on the outages that occurred in the prior generation. To validate the model we compare the distribution of cascade sizes resulting from $n-2$ contingencies in a $2896$ branch test case to cascade sizes in the influence graph. The two distributions are remarkably similar. In addition, we derive an equation with which one can quickly identify modifications to the proposed system that will substantially reduce cascade propagation. With this equation one can quickly identify critical components that can be improved to substantially reduce the risk of large cascading blackouts.

results.title: Voltage Control Partitioning Method Based on Weighted Fast-Newman for Ultra High Voltage Direct Current Power Grid
results.citation: Tianhua Chen et al. (2024) Voltage Control Partitioning Method Based on Weighted Fast-Newman for Ultra High Voltage Direct Current Power Grid doi:10.1109/IFEEA64237.2024.10878526
results.venue: 2024 11th International Forum on Electrical Engineering and Automation (IFEEA)
results.abstract: With the infeed UHV-DC merges into the power grid, the division of voltage control partitioning is more challenging, which is an important step in the secondary voltage control of power system. In this paper, the infeed UHV-DC node is equivalent to a reactive power supply, the weighted Fast-Newman algorithm is used to merge the reactive power sources through the maximum distance difference degree. The proposed method makes load nodes be divided with voltage reactive sensitivity and the average power factor. Which not only reflects the power grid by the center of hub AC bus control requirements, and also conforms to the principle of power grid control hierarchical control. By using the method the control partition result is more adaptable and the influence of UHV-DC transmission power of the partition is considered as well. The rationality of the proposed method is verified by the simulation results of IEEE39 node system.

papergraph_input.title: Voltage Control Partitioning Method Based on Weighted Fast-Newman for Ultra High Voltage Direct Current Power Grid
papergraph_input.citation: Tianhua Chen et al. (2024) Voltage Control Partitioning Method Based on Weighted Fast-Newman for Ultra High Voltage Direct Current Power Grid doi:10.1109/IFEEA64237.2024.10878526
papergraph_input.venue: 2024 11th International Forum on Electrical Engineering and Automation (IFEEA)
papergraph_input.abstract: With the infeed UHV-DC merges into the power grid, the division of voltage control partitioning is more challenging, which is an important step in the secondary voltage control of power system. In this paper, the infeed UHV-DC node is equivalent to a reactive power supply, the weighted Fast-Newman algorithm is used to merge the reactive power sources through the maximum distance difference degree. The proposed method makes load nodes be divided with voltage reactive sensitivity and the average power factor. Which not only reflects the power grid by the center of hub AC bus control requirements, and also conforms to the principle of power grid control hierarchical control. By using the method the control partition result is more adaptable and the influence of UHV-DC transmission power of the partition is considered as well. The rationality of the proposed method is verified by the simulation results of IEEE39 node system.

results.title: Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals
results.citation: Tongbo He et al. (2025) Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals doi:10.1109/ICEEPS66790.2025.11239566
results.venue: 2025 4th International Conference on Energy and Electrical Power Systems (ICEEPS)
results.abstract: As core equipment for grid condition monitoring, power transmission and transformation sensing terminals face critical challenges in ultra-high altitude extreme environments, including material performance degradation, abnormal electrical characteristics, and inadequate operational adaptability. Existing environmental testing standards demonstrate limitations in simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios due to insufficient simulation capabilities and outdated control strategies. This paper proposes an ultra-high altitude adaptability testing methodology for power transmission sensing terminals based on multidimensional environmental coupling loading and dynamic gradient control. Through retrofitting a comprehensive test chamber with integrated ultraviolet irradiation, air circulation, and low-pressure control modules, we established a synchronized multi-environmental factor loading system. The implementation of a pressure-temperature-ultraviolet linkage algorithm coupled with a dynamic gradient control model enables precise simulation of altitude gradient variations. Experimental results demonstrate the method's effectiveness in replicating multidimensional extreme conditions: low pressure (≤70 kPa), intense ultraviolet radiation (≥1000 W/m2), wide temperature fluctuations (-40°C to 70°C), and strong winds (≥15 m/s), while significantly reducing testing cycle duration. This research provides technical support for reliability evaluation of power transmission equipment in ultra-high altitude regions such as the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, demonstrating substantial engineering application value.

results.title: An active de-duplication backup algorithm for storage data in ultra-high-voltage power network based on improved swarm algorithm
results.citation: Ying Zhang (2024) An active de-duplication backup algorithm for storage data in ultra-high-voltage power network based on improved swarm algorithm doi:10.1117/12.3034240
results.venue: Symposium on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering
results.abstract: The source network load and storage data of Ultra High Voltage (UHV) power grid is very large, including massive real-time monitoring data and equipment status data. The backup of these data needs to occupy a lot of storage space, and the transmission and backup process may put forward high requirements on the network bandwidth and transmission speed. Therefore, an active de-duplication backup algorithm based on improved swarm algorithm is proposed for UHV power grid source data storage. The bee colony algorithm is improved and optimized as artificial bee colony algorithm. Simhash algorithm is introduced to remove load and storage data of UHV power grid. Based on this, the balanced binary tree is used to realize its backup. The experimental results show that: Compared with the traditional method, when using the research method to backup data, the storage space is minimum, the data deduplication rate is higher than 95%, and the time is always less than 100ms.

results.title: Analysis of the Impact of Transmitter-Side Electrochemical Energy Storage on Recipient-Side Phase Change Failure in Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current Transmission Systems
results.citation: Yiheng Ren (2024) Analysis of the Impact of Transmitter-Side Electrochemical Energy Storage on Recipient-Side Phase Change Failure in Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current Transmission Systems doi:10.1109/EEBDA60612.2024.10485721
results.venue: 2024 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Big Data and Algorithms (EEBDA)
results.abstract: Phase change failure is one of the common faults in UHV DC transmission system, which leads to voltage fluctuation, current inrush and other phenomena, thus causing serious harm to the safe operation of power grid. Studying the influencing factors of phase change failure is a prerequisite for suppressing phase change failure, and electrochemical energy storage at the sending end can suppress voltage fluctuation, mitigate current impact, improve system inertia, and play a certain buffer and auxiliary role in the case of phase change failure at the receiving end. Therefore, this paper establishes a set of UHV DC transmission system model and explores the influencing factors of phase change failure. Based on this model, the influence mechanism of electrochemical energy storage at the sending end on the phase change failure at the receiving end is investigated from the three aspects of output power size, inductance size and energy storage capacity. The results show that: (1) the larger the output force is, the lesser the degree of phase change failure is. (2) The larger the grounding inductance is, the lower the degree of phase change failure is. (3) The larger the energy storage capacity is, the lower the degree of phase change failure is.

results.title: 500kV Ultra-High Voltage Cable Fine Manufacturing Technology Research
results.citation: Tao Zhang et al. (2025) 500kV Ultra-High Voltage Cable Fine Manufacturing Technology Research doi:10.1109/ICEMPE66159.2025.11123085
results.venue: International Conference on Electrical Materials and Power Equipment
results.abstract: Guangzhou Chuting (Suixi) power transmission and transformation project is the first 500kV ultra-high voltage urban power grid (cable) project of China Southern Power Grid, of which $500 \text{kV} 2500 ~\text{mm}^{2}$ ultra-high voltage cable is the longest transmission distance, the highest voltage level and the largest capacity of the onshore ultra-high voltage cable system in China, and it is also the power cable with the highest voltage level of international cross-linked cable. With years of experience in ultra-high voltage cable manufacturing, Qingdao Hanhe Cable Co., Ltd. has refined the connection of key core processes such as conductor stranding, insulation extrusion, wrinkled aluminum sleeve, hot melt adhesive spraying, etc., and made full use of digitalization, intelligence, informatization and other management methods to successfully complete the project product manufacturing. The completion of this project reflects the ability of localized 500kV ultra-high voltage cable, which is of great significance to promoting the high-end development of China's cable project.

results.title: Research on a Four-Layer Intelligent Monitoring and Coordination Model for Ultra-High Voltage Transmission and Substation Stations in Xinjiang
results.citation: Jiangtao Guo et al. (2025) Research on a Four-Layer Intelligent Monitoring and Coordination Model for Ultra-High Voltage Transmission and Substation Stations in Xinjiang doi:10.1109/ICEPG67373.2025.11466551
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
results.abstract: In response to the unique challenges posed by the rapid expansion of Xinjiang's ultra-high voltage (UHV) power transmission and transformation network, including vast geographical areas, harsh environmental conditions, and a high proportion of renewable energy, this paper proposes a four-layer intelligent monitoring and collaborative framework for future ultra-high voltage digital substations. The proposed framework integrates the perception layer, the edge computing layer, the intelligent analysis layer, and the collaborative decision-making layer. The objective is to achieve multi-source data collection, real-time energy flow visualization, predictive maintenance, resilience assessment, and multi-station collaborative control. The perception layer deploys a distributed sensor network to facilitate continuous monitoring. The edge computing layer undertakes low-latency local processing and anomaly detection. The intelligent analysis layer employs machine learning models to calculate health indices, forecast loads and optimize operations. The collaborative decision-making layer facilitates cross-station coordination, scheduling and emergency management. This framework provides a comprehensive technical and functional reference for the large-scale deployment of digital substation technology in Xinjiang and similar regions.

results.title: Study on the Safety of Power Frequency Electro-Magnetic Exposure from Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines
results.citation: Ji-kun Guo (2024) Study on the Safety of Power Frequency Electro-Magnetic Exposure from Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines doi:10.1109/APPEEC61255.2024.10922335
results.venue: IEEE PES Asia-Pacific Power and Energy Engineering Conference
results.abstract: To prevent potential electromagnetic hazards associated with electrical equipment, the method of employing electromag-netic exposure limits is a widely accepted technical specification across various countries. This study utilizes a 1:1 human body model positioned under a 550 kV ultra-high voltage transmission line to in-vestigate the effects of electromagnetic exposure at different locations. The results indicate that within the power frequency electromagnetic influence zone of the transmission line, there are instances where exposure levels approach or exceed the 10 kV/m limit. However, due to the shielding effect of the human body's weak conductivity, the maximum induced electric field strength and current density at various body parts from the 550 kV ultra-high voltage transmission line were found to be only 62.665 mV/m and 487.943 11 Alm2, respectively. These values are significantly lower than the significant hazard thresholds of 100 m VIm and 2000 11 Alm2 established in biomedical standards. Moreover, as the distance from the center of the transmission lines increases, electromagnetic exposure decays rapidly. There-fore, despite the issue of power frequency electromagnetic exposure from high-voltage and ultra-high voltage transmission lines approaching or exceeding technical regulation limits, the actual harm to humans remains relatively low.

results.title: Optimization of Layout Configuration of Ultra-high Voltage Transmission Lines Based on Mixed Integer Programming
results.citation: Xingang Wang et al. (2025) Optimization of Layout Configuration of Ultra-high Voltage Transmission Lines Based on Mixed Integer Programming doi:10.1109/ACPEE64358.2025.11041176
results.venue: 2025 10th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
results.abstract: China's energy production and consumption are distributed in the opposite direction, generally with a “west-to-east, north-to-south and multiple-energy complement” power distribution pattern. By 2035 and 2050, China's interregional and interprovincial power flows are expected to increase from 250MW in 2020 to 550MW and 740MW, respectively. Therefore, as a backbone channel for transmitting clean energy, the construction of UHV DC transmission channels based on clean energy will be coordinated and promoted. Through the establishment of an optimization model for the layout and configuration of UHV DC transmission lines based on mixed integer planning, the UHV DC transmission lines will be optimally configured by comprehensively considering constraints such as transmission capacity, transmission distance, transmission direction, installed renewable energy potential and power supply structure. It is planned to put 26 new UHV interregional DC transmission lines into operation during 2024–2040, further expanding the capacity and scope of clean energy transmission and consumption, supporting China's clean and low-carbon energy upgrading and transformation, and realising the “3060 Double Carbon” goal.

results.title: Safety Management of Ultra-high Voltage Substation Equipment Operation
results.citation: Jinghui Liu et al. (2024) Safety Management of Ultra-high Voltage Substation Equipment Operation doi:10.62051/ijmee.v4n1.07
results.venue: International Journal of Mechanical and Electrical Engineering
results.abstract: Ultra-high voltage substation plays an important role in the modern power system. It is the transmission hub of power, connecting large power plants and end users. With the continuous expansion of power grid scale and the improvement of voltage level, the safety management of ultra-high voltage substation equipment operation is particularly important. Based from the characteristics of ultra-high voltage substation, this paper will systematically study and discuss the key links of equipment operation safety management, in order to provide useful reference for improving the safe and stable operation level of China's power system.

results.title: Stochastic Dynamic Economic Dispatch Models of Ultra High Voltage AC-DC Hybrid Grids Based on Approximate Dynamic Programming
results.citation: Lang Zhao et al. (2024) Stochastic Dynamic Economic Dispatch Models of Ultra High Voltage AC-DC Hybrid Grids Based on Approximate Dynamic Programming doi:10.1109/epee63731.2024.10875448
results.venue: 2024 4th International Conference on Energy, Power and Electrical Engineering (EPEE)
results.abstract: For the ultra high voltage (UHV) AC-DC hybrid grid, the network losses generated on the transmission channel can be reduced by optimizing the active power allocation of the inter-provincial AC-DC transmission channel. An optimal power allocation model for the transmission channel of the UHV AC/DC hybrid grid is established in this paper to improve the system operation economy. And the state-space approximate dynamic programming method is used to solve the model. It provides a guiding significance for the preparation of contact line power plan of AC-DC hybrid power grid.It provides guiding significance for the preparation of contact line power plan of ACDC hybrid power grid.It can provide guidance for the preparation of contact line power plan of AC-DC hybrid power grid.

results.title: Modeling and Enhancement of Seismic Resilience for Ultra High Voltage Converter Station
results.citation: Zhihang Xue et al. (2024) Modeling and Enhancement of Seismic Resilience for Ultra High Voltage Converter Station doi:10.1109/ICPEE64457.2024.10949981
results.venue: 2024 8th International Conference on Power and Energy Engineering (ICPEE)
results.abstract: The power system is a critical infrastructure for the development of the national economy, and earthquakes pose a significant threat to the operational safety of the power grid. As a key node for long-distance electrical power transmission, the ability of ultra-high voltage (UHV) converter stations to withstand earthquake and recover swiftly afterward has garnered considerable attention within the industry. This article focuses on the modeling and enhancement methods for the seismic resistance of UHV converter station. Firstly, a component diagram of the UHV converter stations is established, and the post-earthquake transmission function index for individual station is defined. Subsequently, based on seismic intensity data, the failure probability of equipment within each branch at a specified seismic intensity is calculated. Additionally, the seismic resilience index R of the UHV converter station is defined, considering the post-earthquake recovery process. Finally, using data from a specific ±800 kV UHV converter station as a case study, its seismic resilience is assessed. Through comparative analysis, methods to enhance seismic resilience are explored. The research holds significant guidance for enhancing the seismic resilience of the UHV voltage converter stations.

results.title: Analysis of Mechanical Properties of High-Voltage Transmission Towers Under Wind Load
results.citation: Ruhai Liu et al. (2025) Analysis of Mechanical Properties of High-Voltage Transmission Towers Under Wind Load doi:10.1109/AEET66561.2025.11307185
results.venue: 2025 International Conference on Applied Electrical Engineering and Technology (AEET)
results.abstract: As the core component of the power system, the high-voltage transmission tower may be damaged in extreme weather conditions such as strong winds, which seriously affects the safety of power grid operation. Aiming at the problem of structural instability and failure caused by wind load during the service of transmission towers, firstly, an accurate three-dimensional mechanical finite element analysis model is established for an ultra-high voltage tower as the research object. Secondly, considering the change of wind speed with the height of the tower, the magnitude and distribution of wind load at different tower heights under the action of $34 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ extreme wind speed are calculated, and the mechanical properties of the tower under the combination of the extreme wind speed and three wind direction angles are studied, and the equivalent stress and deformation law of the tower under different working conditions are calculated. The results show that the combination of $\mathbf{4 5}^{\circ}$ wind direction and $34 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ wind speed is the most unfavorable working condition, under which the top displacement of the tower reaches 97.94 mm, and the maximum stress of the wire cross-bearer is 161.2 MPa, which has reached 46.7 % of the yield strength of Q345 tower steel.

results.title: Synoptic Characteristics of a Continuous Ice Covering Event on Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines in 2023
results.citation: Xinyi Fu et al. (2024) Synoptic Characteristics of a Continuous Ice Covering Event on Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines in 2023 doi:10.1109/eeps63402.2024.10804497
results.venue: 2024 4th International Conference on Energy Engineering and Power Systems (EEPS)
results.abstract: Yunnan is an important channel for the transmission of power from west to east of China Southern Power Grid. During January 14–21, 2023, a large-scale continuous ice covering event occurred in Qujing area of Yunnan Province, with the maximum ice covering thickness of 39.71mm. Based on the reanalysis data of the European Center for Medium Range Weather Prediction (ECMWF) and the observation data of the network of meteorological observation stations, combined with the ice-covering records of ultra-high voltage transmission lines of China Southern Power Grid, the synoptic causes of the ice-covering of power lines are comprehensively analyzed from the aspects of weather situation, atmospheric stratification, local meteorological elements and atmospheric circulation index. The results show that during the glaciating process, the Okhotsk Sea cold vortex is maintained and the transverse trough is turned to vertical. The blocking high leads the northerly dry and cold air in front of the ridge to move southward. Under the influence of the western Pacific tropical high and the southern branch trough, the warm and wet air in front of the rear ridge meets the cold and warm air in front of the ridge. The advance and retreat of the quasi-stationary front determines the icing process of the wire. That is, when the cold air is strong and full of water vapor, the overcooled raindrops freeze or fog drops condenses to form the icing process of the wire. The ice on the wires melts naturally under the warm air mass.

results.title: Analysis of Lightning Strike Faults in Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines and Research on Treatment Measures
results.citation: Qian Zhao et al. (2024) Analysis of Lightning Strike Faults in Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines and Research on Treatment Measures doi:10.1109/MCTE62870.2024.11117782
results.venue: 2024 7th International Conference on Mechatronics and Computer Technology Engineering (MCTE)
results.abstract: This paper analyzes the causes of lightning faults in ultra-high voltage transmission lines of the power grid, including discharge traces and channels, grounding resistance detection, lightning resistance level simulation, and lightning resistance level calculation, in order to obtain the causes of lightning faults. Based on the analysis of lightning faults, six governance measures to avoid lightning faults are proposed, providing reference for reducing the frequency of lightning faults in ultra-high voltage transmission lines.

results.title: Consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles for comprehensive fault diagnosis in series-compensated ultra-high-voltage power systems
results.citation: Rab Nawaz et al. (2026) Consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles for comprehensive fault diagnosis in series-compensated ultra-high-voltage power systems doi:10.1038/s41598-026-49195-7
results.venue: Scientific Reports
results.abstract: Modern power systems incorporate ultra-high-voltage transmission lines with series compensation to enhance their reliability and stability. These configurations generate complex data patterns in electrical signals, which present challenges for accurate fault diagnosis. This complexity arises from various potential faults, such as high-impedance faults and inductive-capacitive interactions, compounded by data scarcity owing to privacy and cybersecurity constraints. This study employs a simulation-based analysis to facilitate fault detection, classification, and localization as unified Multi-Classification tasks. The methodology improves the time-frequency domain data through meta-feature engineering and utilizes consensus-based feature selection by combining gradient boosting, variance thresholding, and mutual information. Model learning is conducted using Bayesian optimization within a Stacking Ensemble, integrating Extra Trees, Light Gradient Boosting Machine, and Extreme Gradient Boosting with an optimized Random Forest meta-classifier. The evaluation considers the accuracy, computational time, inference speed, and memory usage under challenging conditions to address practical limitations. The results indicate that the Stacking Ensemble achieves the highest performance despite its greater computational demand. Extra Trees offers the fastest execution but shows decreased accuracy with increased classification complexity. The Light Gradient Boosting Machine and Extreme Gradient Boosting demonstrate optimized performance across tasks owing to enhanced data processing. These findings confirm the practical applicability of the proposed fault diagnosis framework to modern ultra-high-voltage power systems.

results.title: Technical Transformation Scheme and Practice Applied in Ultra-high Share of Renewable Energy Power System at County-level Scale
results.citation: Ping Xiong et al. (2025) Technical Transformation Scheme and Practice Applied in Ultra-high Share of Renewable Energy Power System at County-level Scale doi:10.1109/ICPST65050.2025.11088518
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Power Science and Technology (ICPST)
results.abstract: The rapid integration of variable renewable energy into county-level power grids has introduced significant operational challenges, particularly transmission congestion and transformer overload caused by bi-directional flow. To overcome the restriction of conventional grid reinforcement solutions focused mainly on infrastructure expansion, the work develops an integrated generation-grid-load-storage coordination framework adapted to scenarios with high penetration of renewables. The solution achieves localized energy balance through optimized energy storage allocation and operational modes control, establishing a resilient power grid architecture capable of grid-connected interaction and autonomous islanded operation. Renovation schemes of building a power system with ultra-high renewable penetration ratios are presented, in which technical routes for the coordination framework are investigated. Performance of key technologies, including grid-forming voltage source converters, smooth mode transition, and the collective control of the coordination system in support of the independent operation and stability of a 100% variable inverter-based renewable energy sources power system, is validated.

results.title: Investigation into In Situ Computed Tomography Inspection Technology for Conductor Connection Structure of Ultra-High Voltage Gas-Insulated Transmission Lines
results.citation: Chao Li et al. (2025) Investigation into In Situ Computed Tomography Inspection Technology for Conductor Connection Structure of Ultra-High Voltage Gas-Insulated Transmission Lines doi:10.1134/S1061830925600133
results.venue: Russian journal of nondestructive testing
results.abstract: The conductor connection structure in gas-insulated transmission lines (GIL) is vital for power grid stability. Traditional inspections require power outages or fault signal detection post-failure, lacking real-time internal structure assessment. This paper introduces an in situ computed Tomography (CT) inspection technology for ultra-high voltage GIL conductors, aiming for non-destructive, high-precision, real-time monitoring. It analyzes Yuyang line GIL’s inspection needs and designs a system with a ring-clamping mechanism and adaptable scanning path. A specialized algorithm tackles incomplete data and imaging accuracy issues unique to GIL. Experiments show the technology’s ability to image GIL conductor connections in three-dimensional (3D), detect defects, and deformations, crucial for GIL’s safe operation and maintenance by identifying and preventing potential failures.

results.title: Exploration of Ultra-High-Voltage Alternating Current Power Transmission Technology Standardization for Global Energy Interconnection
results.citation: Zhou Zhou et al. (2021) Exploration of Ultra-High-Voltage Alternating Current Power Transmission Technology Standardization for Global Energy Interconnection doi:10.1109/ACPEE51499.2021.9437070
results.venue: 2021 6th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
results.abstract: The development of Global Energy Interconnection (GEI), which projects to build a globally interconnected power grid to dispatch electricity generated by renewable energy worldwide, has become a strong driving force to the electrical engineering industry. Ultra-High-Voltage (GEI), consists Alternating Current (AC) and Direct Current (DC) technologies establish the backbone of GEI and it plays an important role in GEI, and it significantly promotes the upgrade of high voltage, electronics, materials, information and other related technologies. With its fast development, there is a urgent demand for the standardization of the technologies. In this paper, we would focus on the UHV AC standardization exploration: First, we present a brief introduction of the background. Second, we demonstrate current development situation of UHV AC standards. Third, we explore the requirement of of UHV AC standards with the development of GEI. Forth, we propose the standard system from the four major areas of general and design, equipment and test, construction, and operation. Fifth, an efficient roadmap is given.

results.title: Particle Swarm Optimization with Stagnation Counter Technique for Optimization of Main Installation Materials Cost in Power Transmission and Transformation Projects
results.citation: Yuliang Jiang et al. (2026) Particle Swarm Optimization with Stagnation Counter Technique for Optimization of Main Installation Materials Cost in Power Transmission and Transformation Projects doi:10.1109/ICICACS68679.2026.11578464
results.venue: 2026 4th International Conference on Integrated Circuits and Communication Systems (ICICACS)
results.abstract: The cost-effective power transmission is the bulk movement of electrical energy form generating stations to substations located near demand centers. The long distance using high voltage lines and it serves as the critical bridge in the electrical grid, ensuring that energy produced as efficient, remote location reaches homes and industries. The conductors, insulators, towers and transformers contribute major portion of the total investment in power transmission and transformation projects. Selection of suitable main installation materials configurations is crucial for minimizing project costs while maintaining high transmission efficiency and system reliability. The existing optimization algorithms focus on overall project investment and faced the inefficient energy transmission problem, when the existing algorithm are unable to concentrate the multiple crucial aspects simultaneously and stuck into the local optima. To mitigate this problem, the Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm with Stagnation Counter Technique (SCT) is used proposed. The PSO is used to concentrate the multiple aspects simultaneously based on the iterative search spaces utilizing swarm collective experience recursively. The SCT is used to overcome the premature convergence problem which is occurred by the PSO, by forcing the algorithm to resume the exploration when the progress is stopped. The proposed PSO-SCT framework is achieved better results comparing to some models such as Standard Particle Swarm Optimization (SPSO), Fully Informed Particle Swarm Optimization (FIPSO). Thus, the proposed PSO-SCT based approach optimizes conductor type, conductor cross-sectional area, transformer capacity, tower configuration, and insulation selection to reduce main installation materials cost while satisfying transmission-efficiency and reliability constraints.

results.title: Study on Reactive Power Compensation Scheme of Brazil Potential 1000kV Ultra-High Voltage Alternating Current (UHV AC) Transmission Project
results.citation: Jia Ding et al. (2023) Study on Reactive Power Compensation Scheme of Brazil Potential 1000kV Ultra-High Voltage Alternating Current (UHV AC) Transmission Project doi:10.1109/ACEEE58657.2023.10239663
results.venue: 2023 6th Asia Conference on Energy and Electrical Engineering (ACEEE)
results.abstract: Based on Brazil potential 1000kV UHV AC Transmission Project system scheme from Milagres to Itabira, the generalized natural power, reactive power balance and reactive power demand analysis method for UHV AC transmission project are discussed firstly, then the main principles and the complete and systematic general process of UHV AC reactive power compensation analysis are presented for the first time. Based on which, the charging power of Brazil UHV transmission project and generalized natural power analysis considering reactive power compensation scheme are offered. Finally, combined with the latest engineering practice, the economic analysis of reactive power compensation schemes is carried out.

results.title: Research and Application of Risk Prediction and Assessment Method for Ultra High Voltage Transmission Channels Under Flood Disasters
results.citation: Shiwen Dong et al. (2025) Research and Application of Risk Prediction and Assessment Method for Ultra High Voltage Transmission Channels Under Flood Disasters doi:10.1109/ICEPG67373.2025.11467061
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
results.abstract: Flood disaster is one of the common natural disaster, which will cause damage or even collapse of ultra high voltage(UHV) transmission lines, seriously affecting the safety and stability of the power grid. The prediction and assessment of the risk level of transmission channels are of great significance. In this paper, the $\pm 800 \text{kV}$ and $\pm 1100 \text{kV}$ UHV DC transmission lines in the flood flow area are taken as the research object, and a risk prediction and assessment method for UHV transmission channel under flood disasters is proposed. Firstly, the finite element analysis software SAP2000 was used to establish a three-dimensional finite element analysis model of the transmission tower structure, and then the calculation and application method of flood loads and the standard for the classification of damage levels of the transmission tower were clarified. Further, the pushover analysis of the transmission tower under different flood depths was carried out to obtain the characteristic loads of the flood under different damage levels, and then the possible flood flow velocity and flood density and impact coefficient under different characteristic loads were obtained based on the Mone-Carlo simulation analysis. Finally, as a training set, the XGBOOST machine learning algorithm was used to train the risk damage prediction and evaluation model of the transmission tower under flood disasters. The results show that the risk prediction model of UHV transmission channels under flood disasters proposed in this paper has high accuracy, and the transmission tower damage classification standard has high practicability. Combined with the daily transmission channel maintenance norms, through the prediction and assessment of the risk damage of UHV transmission channels caused by flood disasters, the transmission operation inspection department will be assisted to effectively carry out the pre-disaster prevention and mid-disaster emergency response work of flood disasters. Finally, the flood disasters prevention and reduction ability of the operation and maintenance departmen will be improved.

results.title: A Simulation Study on Grounding Faults in the Metal Shield Layer of High-Voltage Power Cables
results.citation: Zifan Zhang et al. (2025) A Simulation Study on Grounding Faults in the Metal Shield Layer of High-Voltage Power Cables doi:10.1109/ICLCSE66889.2025.11389670
results.venue: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
results.abstract: In the context of accelerating urbanisation and the transformation of energy structures, high voltage power cables have emerged as a pivotal transmission method within contemporary power systems. The operational reliability of these entities has been demonstrated to have a direct impact on grid security and power supply quality. Within cable structures, the metallic shielding layer fulfils a dual function: it both suppresses electromagnetic interference and protects communication facilities, while also providing a safe return path for ground faults and preventing electric shock accidents. The present study investigates the mechanisms and characteristics of short-circuit faults in cable shields. Utilising the electromagnetic transient simulation software PSCAD, a refined cable model incorporating frequency-dependent parameters was constructed. The electrical characteristics and influencing factors of such faults were revealed through analysis of the variation patterns of shield currents and zero-sequence currents under different operating conditions. The present research provides both theoretical and empirical support for the rapid diagnosis and condition assessment in engineering applications.

results.title: Ultra-High Voltage NV Center Magnetic Sensing System Based on Power over Fiber
results.citation: Linfeng Zhan et al. (2025) Ultra-High Voltage NV Center Magnetic Sensing System Based on Power over Fiber doi:10.3390/photonics12111093
results.venue: Photonics
results.abstract: Aiming to address the insulation and power supply challenges faced by electrical measurement in ultra-high voltage (UHV) environments, this study proposes and implements a nitrogen-vacancy (NV) center magnetic sensing system based on Power over Fiber (PoF) technology. The system adopts a high-voltage and low-voltage separation design, realizing the isolated transmission of electrical energy and the reliable recovery of measurement signals through an optical fiber link. The sensing unit on the high-voltage side is composed of NV center sensors, microwave excitation modules, and signal processing modules. Its power supply is provided by an independently developed high-power laser power converter (LPC) assembly via 830 nm optical fiber laser transmission. Under an optical input of 10 W, this assembly can achieve an electrical output of 4.88 W with a conversion efficiency of 48.9%. The experimental results show that the system can operate stably in a simulated UHV environment; by optimizing modulation parameters, the optimal magnetic measurement sensitivity reaches 6.1 nT/Hz1/2. This research provides a safe and reliable solution for the power supply and precise sensing of high-potential side equipment in UHV scenarios, and demonstrates the application potential of PoF technology in advanced sensing for power systems.

Under an optical input of 10 W, this assembly can achieve an electrical output of 4.88 W with a conversion efficiency of 48.9%. The experimental results show that the system can operate stably in a simulated UHV environment; by optimizing modulation parameters, the optimal magnetic measurement sensitivity reaches 6.1 nT/Hz1/2. This research provides a safe and reliable solution for the power supply and precise sensing of high-potential side equipment in UHV scenarios, and demonstrates the application potential of PoF technology in advanced sensing for power systems.

results.title: Study on Ecological Environmental Impact Assessment Index System of UHV AC Transmission and Transformation Engineering-A Case Study of 1000kv UHV Transmission and Transformation Engineering from Zhangbei to Xiongan
results.citation: Jiming Song et al. (2023) Study on Ecological Environmental Impact Assessment Index System of UHV AC Transmission and Transformation Engineering-A Case Study of 1000kv UHV Transmission and Transformation Engineering from Zhangbei to Xiongan doi:10.1051/e3sconf/202337002001
results.venue: E3S Web of Conferences
results.abstract: Ultra-high voltage (UHV) power grid is a new transmission and transformation technology, which has the advantages of large transmission capacity, long transmission distance, low line loss, saving land resources and obvious economic benefits. Taking the Zhangbei-xiongan 1000kv AC high voltage power transmission and transformation project as an example, this paper constructs the eco-environmental index system of the UHV transmission and transformation project by using the pressure-state-response model, and constructs a set of 18 indexes eco-environmental index system, it mainly includes three aspects: the eco-environmental pressure index caused by the construction behavior in different construction stages of power transmission and transformation projects, and the eco-environmental state index given by on-the-spot investigation and study of projects, based on the construction unit on the ecological environment impact management response measures given indicators. The index system provides a basis for comprehensive and systematic evaluation of the impact of power transmission and distribution projects on the ecological environment, and finally draws the corresponding project construction evaluation conclusions. In this paper, the eco-environmental impact assessment index system of power transmission and transformation engineering has certain universality, and it has certain reference and guiding significance for the ecological environment evaluation of power transmission and transformation engineering.

results.title: Generation mechanism and influencing factors of transient overvoltage on ultra high-voltage direct current transmission
results.citation: Weiwei Yao et al. (2025) Generation mechanism and influencing factors of transient overvoltage on ultra high-voltage direct current transmission doi:10.3389/fenrg.2025.1461365
results.venue: Frontiers in Energy Research
results.abstract: The transient overvoltage caused by faults in ultra-high-voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission lines and alternating current (AC) systems can adversely affect system safety and stability. This study theoretically analyzes the transient overvoltage generation mechanism caused by DC single-pole ground faults and typical fault conditions in two different cases by combining the DC line and AC system of the transmitting side. Considering the different generation mechanisms of the two transient overvoltages, the main factors affecting transient overvoltages were determined to be the DC line ground fault location, DC filter main capacitance, AC system short-circuit ratio, and DC transmission power. Finally, through a simulation of UHVDC transmission engineering, the relationship between various influencing factors and overvoltage was obtained, which can provide guidance in transmission engineering UHVDC design.

results.title: The Seismic Response of Ultra-High Voltage Gas-Insulated Transmission Lines
results.citation: Wei Lv et al. (2023) The Seismic Response of Ultra-High Voltage Gas-Insulated Transmission Lines doi:10.1088/1742-6596/2557/1/012065
results.venue: Journal of Physics: Conference Series
results.abstract: As an important part of the substation, the seismic capacity of ultra-high voltage (UHV) electrical equipment is related to the safe and stable operation of the substation and the whole power grid. The UHV gas-insulated transmission lines (GIL) have many structural characteristics, such as long pipeline distance, the large span between supports and the obvious amplification effect of supports. Its natural frequency is close to the predominant frequency of the seismic wave, resulting in its susceptibility to resonance or resonance-like and high seismic susceptibility. In order to analyze the seismic performance of UHV GIL better, the vibration mode decomposition response spectrum method and the dynamic time-history analysis method are used to analyze the seismic response of UHV GIL in this paper. The search for seismic weak positions of GIL is a reference for effectively improving the seismic performance of UHV GIL.

results.title: Active power decoupling scheme of symmetrical LCL structure in single‐phase grid‐connected voltage source inverter for ultra‐high voltage transmission
results.citation: Jiacheng Luo et al. (2023) Active power decoupling scheme of symmetrical LCL structure in single‐phase grid‐connected voltage source inverter for ultra‐high voltage transmission doi:10.1002/cta.3792
results.venue: International journal of circuit theory and applications
results.abstract: The voltage source converter (VSC) is an important component of ultra‐high voltage (UHV) technology, where the former is responsible for converting new energy sources into electricity and the latter enables the transmission of electricity over long distances. However, the VSC has a serious problem of oscillation power under three‐phase unbalance, which comes from the single‐phase voltage source inverter (VSI) that is the basic structure of VSC. So there is always the requirement for active power decoupling (APD). Conventional passive solutions increase the overall size significantly, and existing active solutions either require the addition of a decoupling topology or rely strongly on their own original topology and more complex control. To overcome this drawback, this paper proposes an improved APD method with a combination of topology‐type and control‐type decoupling features for single‐phase VSI based on the symmetrical LCL filter for solving the power oscillation problem of VSC at the source. The proposed structure can decrease common mode interference and can absorb the oscillation power by the original filter capacitors of the LCL filter. Besides, the original circuit components can be reused, and the decoupling is simple enough to achieve APD without affecting the full bridge control. Next, the control is optimized to reduce the compensation error and input voltage. Finally, the experimental device is carried out to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

results.title: Development of High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) Transmission Systems for Efficient Power Transfer
results.citation: Naveen Garg et al. (2025) Development of High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) Transmission Systems for Efficient Power Transfer doi:10.1109/CISES66934.2025.11264962
results.venue: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent and Secure Engineering Solutions (CISES)
results.abstract: The direct transmission systems using the voltage of one of the largest values, HVDC, that allow transmitting electricity with high efficiency and safety over long distances may be considered one of the most important milestones in the history of electricity engineering and development of the electrical power systems. It has a few strengths such as; it has a lower power loss compared with standard alternating current (AC) systems, is more stable on the grid, and has power density. The fundamental units of the HVDC technology are transformer stations, lines and control systems. HVDC converters technology has made the major focus on voltage source converters (VSCs) and line-commutated converters (LCCs) in power conversion between AC and DC to achieve maximum efficiency of the system. Renewable energy: Here HVDC can adopt renewable energy sources like wind power and solar energy to ease transfer of power through modular and long-distance power transmission of remote generation sites to the centre of consumption. Technology HVDC is being developed in the direction of higher converter efficiency, reduced cost and decreased fault tolerance. Enhanced control algorithms and real-time monitoring software guarantee dynamic stability and easy interface with current grid infrastructures. With the information on the cleaner energy future, an even further progress of the HVDC systems that will be incorporated in the world energy transition on the way to more sustainable and resilient electricity power systems is proceeding.

results.title: Ultra-Long Distance Sensing and Deep Learning-Based Condition Assessment Method for Power Transmission Lines
results.citation: Hanghang Zhao et al. (2025) Ultra-Long Distance Sensing and Deep Learning-Based Condition Assessment Method for Power Transmission Lines doi:10.1109/ACPEE64358.2025.11041579
results.venue: 2025 10th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
results.abstract: With the development of smart grids, the safe and stable operation of power transmission lines is particularly important. This paper proposes a condition assessment method for power transmission lines based on distributed optical fiber ultra-long distance sensing technology combined with deep learning. By applying distributed sensing via optical fibers, the method enables the measurement of quantitative properties like temperature, strain, and vibration of real-time monitoring of power transmission lines. and analyzes the monitoring data through deep learning models to accurately assess The status of high-voltage overhead power lines. Through case analysis of line condition assessment, the feasibility of this method is verified, the overall assessment coincidence is 92.31%, and an assessment model considering temporal features is proposed, which has practical value and reference significance. This innovation makes the state assessment more automated and intelligent, reduces the errors of manual analysis, and improves the efficiency and accuracy of the assessment.

results.title: Meteorological Characteristics of a Continuous Ice-Covered Event on Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines in Yunnan Region in 2021
results.citation: Sen He et al. (2024) Meteorological Characteristics of a Continuous Ice-Covered Event on Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines in Yunnan Region in 2021 doi:10.3390/atmos15040389
results.venue: Atmosphere
results.abstract: Yunnan plays a pivotal role in transmitting electricity from west to east within China’s Southern Power Grid. During 7–13 January 2021, a large-scale continuous ice-covering event of ultra-high voltage (UHV) transmission lines occurred in the Qujing area of eastern Yunnan Province. Based on ERA5 reanalysis data and meteorological observation data of UHV transmission line icing in China’s Southern Power Grid, the synoptic causes of the icing are comprehensively analyzed from various perspectives, including weather situations, vertical stratification of temperature and humidity, local meteorological elements, and atmospheric circulation indices. The results indicate a strong East Asian trough and a blocking high directing northern airflow southward ahead of the ridge. Cold air enters the Qujing area and combines with warm and moist air from the subtropical high pressure of 50–110° E. As warm and cold air masses form a quasi-stationary front over the northern mountainous area of Qujing due to topographic uplift, the mechanism of “supercooling and warm rain” caused by the “warm–cold” temperature profile structure leads to freezing rain events. Large-scale circulation indices in the Siberian High, East Asian Trough, and 50–110° E Subtropical High regions provided clear precursor signals within 0–2 days before the icing events.

results.title: Research on project cost prediction of ultra-high voltage transmission lines based on RF-HKELM
results.citation: Jinci Li et al. (2023) Research on project cost prediction of ultra-high voltage transmission lines based on RF-HKELM doi:10.1117/12.3013261
results.venue: International Conference on Internet of Things and Machine Learning
results.abstract: In the context of China's increasing efforts to build extra-high voltage transmission projects, accurate and effective project cost prediction of extra-high voltage transmission lines can not only rationally arrange the investment of grid construction funds, but also effectively improve the operating conditions of power grid enterprises and guarantee the stable development of power grid enterprises. This paper takes data mining as a means to screen out the main factors affecting the cost of ultrahigh voltage transmission project, and screens the factors through random forest, takes the screened factors as inputs and the cost of engineering units as outputs, constructs a cost prediction model based on HKELM neural network, and proves the validity and applicability of the model to the investment prediction of ultra-high voltage transmission line project through empirical research, which provides an opportunity to predict the cost of ultra-high voltage transmission line project for the grid enterprises and to improve the operation of the grid enterprises. It provides a new method for the cost prediction of ultra-high voltage transmission line project.

results.title: Inhibition of Subsequent Commutation Failures in Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current Transmission Systems Using Electrochemical Energy Storage at the Sending End
results.citation: Wenhui Wang et al. (2024) Inhibition of Subsequent Commutation Failures in Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current Transmission Systems Using Electrochemical Energy Storage at the Sending End doi:10.2174/0124055204332396240819071726
results.venue: Recent Innovations in Chemical Engineering (Formerly Recent Patents on Chemical Engineering)
results.abstract:
Subsequent commutation failures (SCFs) in high-voltage direct
current (HVDC) systems pose a serious threat to the safe operation of hybrid AC/DC grids.
Electrochemical energy storage, which is widely distributed at the sending end of ultrahigh
voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission systems, has the potential to mitigate
SCFs. To fully harness the SCF-mitigating capabilities of energy storage, this article first
establishes a CIGRE-HVDC standard test model incorporating electrochemical energy
storage at the sending end.

Based on this model, the factors influencing DC commutation failures are investigated.
Furthermore, the impact of rectifier-side electrochemical energy storage (EES) on
inverter-side commutation failures is explored from three aspects: energy storage capacity,
output magnitude, and fault conditions. It is found that rectifier-side EES absorbing power
can effectively suppress inverter-side commutation failures. Finally, based on this finding,
a transient active power control strategy for energy storage is designed to inhibit consecutive
commutation failures and is studied on the CIGRE-HVDC standard test system. It is
concluded that the optimal capacity for suppressing SCFs is between 20% and 30% of the
DC capacity, and the best absorption power output is achieved with a per-unit value of 1.

Simulation results confirm the correctness of the proposed energy storage transient
active power control strategy and its effectiveness in suppressing SCF under different fault
moments, fault severities, and fault types.

This strategy can limit the number of SCFs to three or fewer in the majority
of operating conditions, facilitating rapid system recovery after faults.

results.title: Construction and Implementation of “Model Room for Ultra High Voltage Important Transmission Channels” Based on Digital Control
results.citation: Jian Song et al. (2024) Construction and Implementation of “Model Room for Ultra High Voltage Important Transmission Channels” Based on Digital Control doi:10.1109/ICPEA63589.2024.10784543
results.venue: 2024 7th International Conference on Power and Energy Applications (ICPEA)
results.abstract: With the increasingly complex operation environment of UHV transmission line channels, and in order to meet the requirements of the construction and development of new power systems and digital and intelligent power grids, State Grid Shanxi UHV Transmission Company has built and implemented a “model room for important UHV transmission channels” based on digital and intelligent control. Firstly, a full-view collaborative three-dimensional inspection system was constructed through technologies such as unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) autonomous inspection and channel visualization. Secondly, improve the transmission panoramic IoT monitoring platform to realize realtime monitoring and intelligent early warning of transmission equipment and channels; Finally, a differentiated management and control system for power grid risk should be established to improve the refinement and efficiency of power grid risk management through accurate assessment and control measures. The implementation results show that the model room has significantly improved the digital intelligence level and management quality and efficiency of transmission operation and maintenance, enhanced the power supply reliability of UHV transmission channels, and achieved significant economic and social benefits. This study provides a practical case and reference for the development of digital intelligence in power grids, and is of great significance for promoting the sustainable and healthy development of UHV power grid construction.

results.title: Research on the Protection Scheme of a High-Speed Railway Crossing 1000 KV Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Line
results.citation: Yi-chun Song et al. (2024) Research on the Protection Scheme of a High-Speed Railway Crossing 1000 KV Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Line doi:10.3390/infrastructures9070110
results.venue: Infrastructures
results.abstract: The high-speed railway project and the ultra-high-voltage transmission project represent two crucial components of China’s “new infrastructure”. As the construction of these two projects progresses rapidly, it is inevitable that instances of intersections will occur. Extreme conditions may cause damage to ultra-high voltage transmission cables. When a high-speed train passes by an ultra-high voltage transmission line, it poses a serious safety hazard. To address this issue, engineering examples were utilized to examine the protection structure scheme, protection distance, protection load, and construction procedures when a high-speed railway intersects a 1000 KV ultra-high voltage transmission line. A shed structure form and construction method for the electric power protection were proposed to ensure the safe operation of the high-speed railway while also achieving the safe and rapid construction of the high-speed railway protection structure in the safety zone of the approaching 1000 kV ultra-high voltage transmission line. The results indicated that the protection of high-speed railway crossings and 1000 kV ultra-high voltage transmission lines primarily focuses on line-break protection. The concrete shed structure with a straight wall and a flat roof was designed to meet the requirements of high-speed railway crossings. The line-break protection method enables the construction of an automatic warning protection corridor and a complete movable trolley quickly and safely within the safety zone near the transmission line. The implementation effect is, therefore, positive. It can be used as a reference point for other projects of a similar nature.

The implementation effect is, therefore, positive. It can be used as a reference point for other projects of a similar nature.

results.title: Transient Voltage Characteristics Analysis of DC Receiving-End Power Grid with Large-Scale Integrated Distributed Photovoltaics
results.citation: Jia Wang et al. (2024) Transient Voltage Characteristics Analysis of DC Receiving-End Power Grid with Large-Scale Integrated Distributed Photovoltaics doi:10.1109/pset62496.2024.10808499
results.venue: 2024 3rd International Conference on Power Systems and Electrical Technology (PSET)
results.abstract: As the construction of ultra-high-voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission projects progresses, more regions are forming receiving-end power grids that integrate new energy sources and DC injection, significantly impacting the transient voltage stability of the power system. To investigate the effects of high proportions of distributed photovoltaic (PV) integration on the stability of the new power system, it is crucial to consider both DC injection and distributed PV integration in the analysis to understand the new characteristics of transient voltage at the receiving end of the grid. Initially, the study focuses on examining the new P-V characteristics of distributed PV systems interconnected with the distribution network at high penetration levels to evaluate the system's transient behavior. Subsequently, by integrating the dynamic characteristics of DC system reactive power after faults in the receiving-end AC system, and using the CIGRE standard test model, the analysis assesses the risks of DC commutation failure and transient voltage instability in the receiving-end AC system under varying distributed PV installation capacities. The results indicate that the strength of the DC receiving-end grid weakens as the PV station integration capacity increases, and under conditions where a three-phase fault occurs at the point of integration, consecutive commutation failures may potentially occur. Finally, leveraging actual data from the Tianzhong DC project in the Henan Power Grid, a receiving-end grid model incorporating the DC transmission system and distributed PV power sources is established. The study investigates the new characteristics of transient voltage at the receiving end of the grid under high proportions of distributed PV integration, confirming that the increasing installation capacity of distributed PV exacerbates the risk of transient voltage instability in the DC receiving-end grid.

results.title: Transformative AI Technologies in High-Voltage Systems: A Review of Advances in Predictive Maintenance, Fault Detection, and Grid Optimization
results.citation: Ian B. Benitez et al. (2025) Transformative AI Technologies in High-Voltage Systems: A Review of Advances in Predictive Maintenance, Fault Detection, and Grid Optimization doi:10.1109/IICAIET67254.2025.11265217
results.venue: International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Engineering and Technology
results.abstract: High voltage engineering has evolved rapidly, driven by the growing need for efficient energy transmission and the integration of renewable energy into modern power grids, including urban areas. Innovations such as HVDC systems are central to this transformation, ensuring that grids can handle the increasing complexity and demand for sustainable energy. However, challenges remain, especially when it comes to coordinating insulation in hybrid AC/DC systems and maintaining the resilience of the overall infrastructure. This review looks at how Artificial Intelligence (AI) can help tackle these challenges, focusing on its role in fault detection, predictive maintenance, and improving system reliability. By comparing traditional methods with AI-driven solutions, we highlight how AI can enhance the scalability, efficiency, and adaptability of power systems. With AI, utilities can predict and prevent faults, optimize grid performance, and seamlessly integrate renewable energy sources into both rural and urban environments. Our goal is to provide insights for researchers, industry professionals, and policymakers on how AI can be harnessed to build more sustainable, resilient, and reliable energy systems. The insights shared here aim to help shape the future of power grids, positioning AI as a key player in the transition to cleaner, more efficient energy solutions.

results.title: Research on Wireless Power Transmission Technology and Its Application in Electrical Engineering
results.citation: Xiaolong Wang et al. (2025) Research on Wireless Power Transmission Technology and Its Application in Electrical Engineering doi:10.1109/ICPICS66386.2025.11347183
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Power, Intelligent Computing and Systems (ICPICS)
results.abstract: In order to improve the flexibility and safety of power supply of electrical engineering systems in scenarios such as high-voltage isolation, complex environments and mobile devices, this paper studies wireless power transmission technology and its application in smart substations, comprehensively and systematically sorts out three types of wireless power supply mechanisms: electromagnetic induction, magnetic resonance coupling and microwave transmission, builds a wireless power supply system framework with magnetic resonance coupling as the core, and deploys the system in 110 kV smart substation scenarios. Test results show that the system achieves an average transmission efficiency of 78.6 % at a coupling distance of 2.8 meters, controls the power fluctuation rate within plus or minus 2.6 %, and reduces the annual interruption frequency to 3 times. The comprehensive operation stability and safety far exceed the traditional wired method. Wireless power transmission shows good engineering practice feasibility and expansion potential, and is suitable for key parts in smart grids that have high requirements for flexibility, safety and efficiency of energy supply.

results.title: NV centers magnetic sensing system in ultra-high-voltage utilizing power over fiber
results.citation: Xiaofei Li et al. (2026) NV centers magnetic sensing system in ultra-high-voltage utilizing power over fiber doi:10.1088/1742-6596/3174/1/012058
results.venue: Journal of Physics, Conference Series
results.abstract: This paper proposes and experimentally studies a magnetic sensing system that employs nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centers in diamond and utilizes power over fiber (PoF) technology for ultra-high voltage (UHV) scenarios. The structure and working principle of the system are introduced. The designed PoF link delivers a maximum power output of 3.8 W with an electrical transmission efficiency of 38%, while the designed sensor exhibits outstanding electromagnetic stability within the 2.867 GHz frequency band. Finally, the system ultimately attains a sensitivity of 6.1 nT/Hz1/2 for magnetic measurements.

results.title: An IoT-based quality inspection robot for measuring conductor sag and detecting defects in ultra-high voltage overhead transmission lines
results.citation: Jiayin Song et al. (2025) An IoT-based quality inspection robot for measuring conductor sag and detecting defects in ultra-high voltage overhead transmission lines doi:10.1088/1361-6501/adb2ac
results.venue: Measurement science and technology
results.abstract: Aiming to solve the urgent problem of sag measurement in constructing ultra-high voltage (UHV) power transmission lines non-line-of-sight conditions, we have developed an internet of things-based quality inspection robot for UHV overhead transmission line conductors to assist in construction. This robot not only improves the accuracy of sag measurement but also possesses the function of detecting and locating line defects. Utilizing sensors, it monitors sag, environment, and operating conditions in real-time and uploads the results to a cloud platform. The proposed lightweight YOLOv5s-SGSW algorithm, deployed on NVIDIA Jetson TX2, detects transmission line defects with 81.9% precision at 35.62 FPS. The integration of autonomous positioning algorithms and defect detection locates the defects on the transmission line. The relative errors are 0.25% for the X-coordinate and 2.07% for the Y-coordinate of the defect localization. A new tension-based sag compensation method is proposed to compensate for the sag error caused by the robot’s weight, with experimental results showing an error of 1.3%. This indicates that the robot can meet the expected design goals and satisfy the practical engineering requirements.

results.title: Reinforcement Learning-Integrated Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Path Optimization and Dynamic Pricing Mechanism Design
results.citation: Xingqiang Wang et al. (2025) Reinforcement Learning-Integrated Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Path Optimization and Dynamic Pricing Mechanism Design doi:10.1109/ICIPS67876.2025.11331937
results.venue: 2025 5th International Conference on Intelligent Power and Systems (ICIPS)
results.abstract: Ultra-high voltage transmission systems, as the backbone of modern power networks, face dual challenges in path optimization and electricity pricing mechanism design. This paper proposes an ultra-high voltage transmission path optimization method that integrates deep reinforcement learning, combined with dynamic pricing mechanisms to achieve optimal overall system benefits. The study constructs a path selection model based on Q-learning algorithm and designs a dynamic electricity price adjustment mechanism considering load fluctuations and network topology changes. Through simulation experiments, the effectiveness of the proposed method in reducing transmission losses and improving system stability is validated. Experimental results show that this method can reduce transmission losses by 15.6% and improve overall system economic benefits by 12.3%, providing a new technical pathway for intelligent operation of ultra-high voltage power grids.
