results.title: Development and Experimental Study of De-Icing Short-Circuit Device for Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines Based on Rapid Clamping Structure
results.citation: Jinpei Wang et al. (2026) Development and Experimental Study of De-Icing Short-Circuit Device for Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines Based on Rapid Clamping Structure doi:10.1109/ICSGGE69348.2026.11508193
results.venue: 2026 5th International Conference on Smart Grid and Green Energy (ICSGGE)
results.abstract: To address the issues of inconvenient installation and poor adaptability associated with traditional fixed crimping drainage lines during sectionalized direct-current (DC) icemelting of transmission lines, this paper aims to develop a novel phase-to-phase short-circuit device for conductor ice-melting. The device adopts a “T-shaped” aluminum alloy body and a modular quick-press cover plate. It significantly enhances heat dissipation performance by utilizing the reserved air convection gaps. Through a highly reliable hinge and a quick-pressing mechanism, it achieves non-pressurized quick assembly and disassembly and stable connection with the wires. Experimental results demonstrate that under the rated current required by engineering standards, the steady-state temperature rise of the device is lower than that of the conductor. Furthermore, the ratio of its DC resistance to that of an equal-length conductor is less than 1.1, satisfying standard requirements. Characterized by its lightweight structure, convenient installation, and reliable current-carrying capability, the device flexibly adapts to various bundle conductors and two-phase/three-phase short-circuiting scenarios. It significantly enhances the efficiency and safety of emergency icemelting operations, providing effective technical equipment for the power grid to mitigate icing hazards.

results.title: Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering
results.citation: D. Danylchenko et al. (2025) Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering doi:10.20998/eree.2025.1(10).333342
results.venue: Bulletin of the National Technical University "KhPI". Series: Energy: Reliability and Energy Efficiency
results.abstract: The article presents a comprehensive study of China's energy security from a technical and economic perspective, taking into account current challenges and strategic goals of the energy transition. The main focus is on the quantitative analysis of structural problems that threaten the stability of the energy system. In particular, based on 2023 data, it was found that the structure of energy consumption remains dependent on fossil fuels: the share of coal is 56.4 %, and oil import dependence is 72.6 %. In addition, the probability of overloading the power grid in extreme weather conditions was found to be 7.3 %, indicating the system's vulnerability to climate change. The study modeled the effectiveness of the implementation of Smart Grid technologies, which increased the efficiency of ultra-high voltage power transmission by 18 %. The study also recorded a 40 % increase in the total capacity of virtual power plants and an increase in the internal rate of return of energy storage systems based on lithium batteries to 8.7 %. From the perspective of long-term strategic planning, the proposed multi-energy flow model estimates the cost of transporting 500 km of green hydrogen at 35 yuan/kg, and also forms a standardized power compensation mechanism (0.08 yuan/kW). The empirical results confirm the feasibility of such measures: the rate of rejection of new transmission lines in the northwestern regions has been reduced to 3.2 %, and the introduction of 12 GW of energy storage systems in the Yangtze Delta has reduced peak load by 15%. It is also demonstrated that the cost of hydrogen production from offshore wind energy has reached 0.3 yuan/kWh, or 35 yuan/kg, taking into account market electricity tariffs. The article proposes strategic measures to ensure energy security, including investments in scientific and technological development in the amount of 0.8 % of gross domestic product, modernization of the power grid with funding of 4.2 trillion yuan over the next 10 years, and the creation of an adaptive political monitoring system. These results form the basis for a systematic electrical engineering approach to ensuring China's energy sustainability in the context of global transformations.

results.title: Analysis and Improvement Measures of Current Limiting Resistance Value in Multi Circuit Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current System with Common Ground Electrode
results.citation: Dongxu Deng et al. (2025) Analysis and Improvement Measures of Current Limiting Resistance Value in Multi Circuit Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current System with Common Ground Electrode doi:10.1109/IEEECONF68944.2025.11398619
results.venue: 2025 2nd international conference on DC technologies and systems (DCTS)
results.abstract: Currently, ultra-high voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission systems are developing rapidly. From an economic perspective, when multiple UHVDC systems share a common grounding electrode, excessive grounding current may lead to grid failures. By analyzing the role of current-limiting resistors in multi-circuit UHVDC systems, this study proposes solutions for the calculation and improved design of current-limiting resistors based on current technologies and advancements in power transmission.

results.title: Simulating the mode of one-sided switching-on of an ultra-high voltage power line using a device for synchronizing the moments of switching-on the contacts of the circuit breaker phases
results.citation: S. Morozov et al. (2023) Simulating the mode of one-sided switching-on of an ultra-high voltage power line using a device for synchronizing the moments of switching-on the contacts of the circuit breaker phases doi:10.30987/2658-6436-2023-2-89-96
results.venue: Automation and modeling in design and management of
results.abstract: Simulating the switching on of an overhead power transmission line (PTL) of ultra-high voltage in a dead-end mode, containing a significant aperiodic current component, both from the switch control key and in the three-phase automatic reclosing (TARC) cycle, is performed using the MATLAB software package. The aim of modelling is to search and find the optimal modes of electrical transmission operation in terms of admitting the values of the aperiodic current component and assessing possible values of overvoltages in the phases of power transmission lines. The possibility of using phase-by-phase control of a high-voltage circuit breaker, which ensures closing the power contacts of the circuit breaker phase at the moment of the maximum value of the instantaneous voltage on these contacts, is checked as a measure that can significantly reduce the influence of the aperiodic current component on the possibility of damaging switching equipment and developing an emergency situation in the power system. The results obtained can be used to practically eliminate the influence of the aperiodic current component in all possible modes (dead-end mode of an overhead line (OL), in an unsuccessful automatic reclosing cycle (ARC) for all types of short circuits on a high-voltage power line) with shunt reactors put into operation and without putting them into operation.

results.title: Research on Reactive Power and Voltage Layered and Partitioned Collaborative Control Strategy for Suppressing the Chain Evolution of Large Scale New Energy DC Transmission
results.citation: Tao Sun et al. (2025) Research on Reactive Power and Voltage Layered and Partitioned Collaborative Control Strategy for Suppressing the Chain Evolution of Large Scale New Energy DC Transmission doi:10.1109/ETEP67941.2025.11440636
results.venue: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
results.abstract: Large scale new energy ultra-high voltage direct current transmission, due to the low short-circuit ratio of the transmission end power grid and the strong impact of DC commutation failure, causes significant transient voltage fluctuations along the propagation path of the transmission end power grid from the DC rectifier station to the new energy cluster stations to the new energy collection stations, which in turn triggers a chain evolution of new energy high and low voltage crossing protection actions to disconnect from the grid, impacting the safety of the power grid. This article proposes a reactive power voltage layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy for multiple reactive power sources in DC rectifier station, new energy cluster stations, and new energy collection stations to prevent and control this problem. Considering the time delay of SVG power electronic response control in new energy collection stations, an SVG high and low voltage open-loop control strategy is proposed. Compared with closed-loop PID control, it can enhance the timeliness of control and effectively suppress the rapid transient voltage fluctuations caused by commutation failure. At the same time, the simulation analysis of the layered and partitioned dynamic support and rapid regulation effect provided by the rectifier station synchronous condenser and the thermal power units in the near area of new energy cluster stations is conducted. Based on simulation examples of actual power grids, the effectiveness of the proposed multi reactive power source layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy in suppressing the cascading evolution problem of the transmission end power grid has been verified, improving the DC transmission capacity and promoting the consumption of new energy.

papergraph_input.citation: Tao Sun et al. (2025) Research on Reactive Power and Voltage Layered and Partitioned Collaborative Control Strategy for Suppressing the Chain Evolution of Large Scale New Energy DC Transmission doi:10.1109/ETEP67941.2025.11440636
papergraph_input.venue: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
papergraph_input.abstract: Large scale new energy ultra-high voltage direct current transmission, due to the low short-circuit ratio of the transmission end power grid and the strong impact of DC commutation failure, causes significant transient voltage fluctuations along the propagation path of the transmission end power grid from the DC rectifier station to the new energy cluster stations to the new energy collection stations, which in turn triggers a chain evolution of new energy high and low voltage crossing protection actions to disconnect from the grid, impacting the safety of the power grid. This article proposes a reactive power voltage layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy for multiple reactive power sources in DC rectifier station, new energy cluster stations, and new energy collection stations to prevent and control this problem. Considering the time delay of SVG power electronic response control in new energy collection stations, an SVG high and low voltage open-loop control strategy is proposed. Compared with closed-loop PID control, it can enhance the timeliness of control and effectively suppress the rapid transient voltage fluctuations caused by commutation failure. At the same time, the simulation analysis of the layered and partitioned dynamic support and rapid regulation effect provided by the rectifier station synchronous condenser and the thermal power units in the near area of new energy cluster stations is conducted. Based on simulation examples of actual power grids, the effectiveness of the proposed multi reactive power source layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy in suppressing the cascading evolution problem of the transmission end power grid has been verified, improving the DC transmission capacity and promoting the consumption of new energy.

results.title: A Novel Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current Line Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Principal Component Analysis and Kernel Density Estimation
results.citation: Haojie Zhang et al. (2025) A Novel Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current Line Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Principal Component Analysis and Kernel Density Estimation doi:10.3390/s25030642
results.venue: Italian National Conference on Sensors
results.abstract: As renewable energy resources are increasingly deployed on a large scale in remote areas, their share within the power grid continues to expand, rendering direct current (DC) transmission essential to the stability and efficiency of power systems. However, existing transmission line protection principles are constrained by limited fault feature quantities and insufficient correlation exploration among features, leading to operational refusals under remote and high-resistance fault conditions. To address these limitations in traditional protection methods, this study proposes an innovative single-ended protection principle based on Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and Kernel Density Estimation (KDE). Initially, PCA is employed for multidimensional feature extraction from fault data, followed by KDE to construct a joint probability density function of the multidimensional fault features, allowing for fault type identification based on the joint probability density values of new samples. In comparison to conventional methods, the proposed approach effectively uncovers intrinsic correlations among multidimensional features, integrating them into a comprehensive feature set for fault diagnosis. Simulation results indicate that the method exhibits robustness across various transition resistances and fault distances, demonstrates insensitivity to sampling frequency, and achieves 100% accuracy in fault identification across sampling time windows of 0.5 ms, 1 ms, and 2 ms.

results.title: Analysis of the Influence of UHV AC Access on Power Transmission and Receiving Capability of Provincial Power Grid
results.citation: Xiaofan Su et al. (2024) Analysis of the Influence of UHV AC Access on Power Transmission and Receiving Capability of Provincial Power Grid doi:10.1109/iciba62489.2024.10867916
results.venue: 2024 IEEE 4th International Conference on Information Technology, Big Data and Artificial Intelligence (ICIBA)
results.abstract: With the increasing scale of UHV DC infeed and the large-scale development of renewable energy in the province, the AC-DC hybrid characteristics of the power grid are more complex. After the ultra-high voltage DC bipolar blocking and transferred to the inter-regional connecting line, the power shortage is reduced, and the coupling characteristics between the 500kV regional interconnection line are weakened. The maximum inter-regional power flow and the receiving power of the ultra-high voltage DC are increased after the ultra-high voltage AC network is built. The existing problems of the actual provincial power grid are analyzed. Then, based on the UHV AC drop point, the power flow change of the main 500kV transmission line in the provincial power grid is analyzed under the access system scheme and different operation modes. Finally, from the aspect of improving the overall external power-receiving capability of the power grid, the improvement level of the power-receiving capability of the provincial power grid after UHV AC operation under different schemes is compared and analyzed.

results.title: Synoptic Characteristics and Ice Prediction Model Test Based on an Ice Event of Ultra-High-Voltage Transmission Lines in the Northwest of Guangdong in 2022
results.citation: Zhou Zhenzhen et al. (2023) Synoptic Characteristics and Ice Prediction Model Test Based on an Ice Event of Ultra-High-Voltage Transmission Lines in the Northwest of Guangdong in 2022 doi:10.1109/ICCCBDA56900.2023.10154720
results.venue: 2023 8th International Conference on Cloud Computing and Big Data Analytics (ICCCBDA)
results.abstract: This February 2022 is a very serious ice-covering of ultra-high voltage transmission lines in Guangdong area, with a maximum ice-covering thickness of 32.68 mm. In this paper, using ERA5 reanalysis information and ice-covering monitoring system information provided by the Southern Power Grid, we discuss the causes of this ice-covering in depth from the weather circulation situation, temperature and humidity vertical layer changes, and meteorological conditions, and compare and analysis the ice-covering forecast model prediction results with the actual ice-covering data. It is finally found that the effect of cold vortex in northeast region and western Pacific vice high makes the horizontal trough turn vertical, guiding cold air southward and converging with wet air in Guangdong, which is favorable for ice cover generation; the results of model comparison show that the temperature decreases rapidly from above 0 to below 0, which is easy to generate ice cover, and the model forecast results match with the actual ice cover process, and the error comes from the unstable forecast accuracy of meteorological elements temperature and wind speed.

results.title: Key Technology of Relay Protection and Setting Calculation of Uhv Power Grid
results.citation: Jinghui Liu et al. (2024) Key Technology of Relay Protection and Setting Calculation of Uhv Power Grid doi:10.62051/ijmee.v4n1.08
results.venue: International Journal of Mechanical and Electrical Engineering
results.abstract: As the core component of the modern power transmission system, the stable operation of the UHV power grid is of great significance to ensure the overall safety of the power system. As a key line of defense in the ultra-high voltage power grid, relay protection can detect and isolate faults in time, thus limiting the impact of the accident to the minimum range. Setting calculation is an important basis for relay protection, which involves a series of complex calculation and analysis processes. This paper deeply analyzes the key technology of ultra high voltage power grid relay protection setting calculation, discusses the specific factors of the substation configuration on the setting calculation, carefully analyzes the principles of the setting calculation, comprehensively analyzes the related protection device configuration strategy, and further study the potential problems existing in the setting calculation and its effective countermeasures. Through the in-depth discussion and research of these key technical problems, it provides a set of scientific and reasonable norms and guidance methods for the relevant personnel in the setting and calculation of the relay protection of the ultra-high voltage power grid, so as to effectively guarantee the long-term stable operation and safe power supply of the power system.

results.title: Power grid practice of suppressing transient overvoltage of sending-end system by ultra high voltage direct current control parameters
results.citation: Mingkai Yang et al. (2022) Power grid practice of suppressing transient overvoltage of sending-end system by ultra high voltage direct current control parameters doi:10.1088/1742-6596/2310/1/012036
results.venue: Journal of Physics: Conference Series
results.abstract: The transient overvoltage off-grid problem of new energy in the sending-end network has become an important factor limiting the ultra high voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission capacity. In this paper, the causes of transient overvoltage during the DC fault process is analysed firstly, then from the perspective of adjusting the reactive power consumption of the converter, taking the Qingyu UHVDC project in China as an actual simulation example, the influence of the DC control mode and current control parameters at the rectifier side on the transient overvoltage is deeply studied. Combined with the transient voltage evaluation results of the actual power grid, guiding suggestions for DC control strategies are given to improve the transmission capacity. The research results have important reference significance for improving the consumption of new energy and the transmission capacity of UHVDC projects.

results.title: Transient Overvoltage Analysis and Insulation Coordination for an ±800 kV/8 GW MMC-Based Ultra-High-Voltage DC Transmission System
results.citation: Xiaorui Liu et al. (2026) Transient Overvoltage Analysis and Insulation Coordination for an ±800 kV/8 GW MMC-Based Ultra-High-Voltage DC Transmission System doi:10.3390/electronics15132859
results.venue: Electronics
results.abstract: Facing the demand of long-distance, high-voltage and high-power transmission, the research on MMC-UHVDC (Modular Multilevel Converter based Ultra High Voltage Direct Current) has become a hot issue. This paper focuses on the ±800 kV/8 GW UHVDC transmission system to conduct simulation modelling and insulation coordination studies. First, broadband models of the main circuit and primary equipment are established to simulate and analyse the distribution characteristics of both switching and lightning transient overvoltages under typical faults. Second, based on the overvoltage severity at critical nodes, two surge arrester configuration schemes with distinct internal valve protection topologies are proposed. Finally, an Improved Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (FAHP) is introduced to perform a quantitative techno-economic evaluation of the comparative schemes. The results demonstrate that the optimised configuration successfully suppresses extreme overvoltages at vulnerable sub-module nodes, maintaining adequate insulation margins. These research findings provide a highly reliable mathematical framework and engineering reference for the safe design of UHVDC systems.

results.title: Voltage Transient Analysis and Control Discussion of a Power Grid with High Proportion of Photovoltaic and Large-Capacity DC Receiving
results.citation: Tiantian Li et al. (2024) Voltage Transient Analysis and Control Discussion of a Power Grid with High Proportion of Photovoltaic and Large-Capacity DC Receiving doi:10.1109/ACFPE63443.2024.10801130
results.venue: 2024 3rd Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
results.abstract: With the advancement of ultra-high voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission projects, an increasing number of regions are developing receiving-end grids that integrate both renewable energy and DC power input, significantly impacting the transient voltage stability of power systems. To investigate the effects of high-penetration distributed photovoltaic (PV) integration on the stability of modern power systems, both DC transmission and distributed PV systems are considered in the analysis to understand the new transient voltage characteristics of the receiving-end grid. In response to two key issues: insufficient reactive power support from the receiving-end AC system under high-penetration distributed PV conditions, leading to repeated commutation failures in the DC transmission system and the risk of transient voltage instability in the AC system, this paper proposes a coordinated transient voltage control method for power systems with high penetration of distributed PV and energy storage. By monitoring voltage variations at renewable energy integration points and estimating the required reactive power compensation based on the short-circuit ratios of distributed renewable energy and collection points, the method enhances transient voltage stability. Coordinated control of PV and energy storage is employed, taking into account different PV reactive power margins, to develop the reactive power compensation capability of PV inverters and ensure smooth power output, ultimately improving the transient voltage stability at grid connection points.

results.title: CNN-Based Digital Twin Model for Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current System Loss Measurement
results.citation: Xiaodong Yin et al. (2024) CNN-Based Digital Twin Model for Ultra-High Voltage Direct Current System Loss Measurement doi:10.1109/ACCESS.2024.3402386
results.venue: IEEE Access
results.abstract: The ultra-high voltage direct current (UHVDC) system is widely constructed due to its suitability for large-capacity long-distance transmission, and its losses have become an important part of the power grid losses. However, the overall and subcomponent losses of the power system are difficult to measure by measuring devices or accurately calculated by existing algorithms. In this paper, a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based digital twin model for UHVDC system loss measurement is proposed. The parameters and data of the physical system are fed into the digital space system through the data interaction. In the digital twin model of the digital space system, a CNN network with added topology information extraction layers and key equipment parameter adaptive perception layers excavates the deep multidimensional correlation features of the digital space mapping data. Then a multi-task joint processing layer fuses the extracted deep features and missing information to calculate the total and sub-component losses. The training and testing results based on actual UHVDC project data show that the mean absolute error (MAE) is 0.9, while R2 is 0.9999, which proves the accuracy of the proposed digital twin model is superior to mainstream deep learning models. This model is embedded in digital twin devices and applied in actual UHVDC converter stations.

results.title: Research on the Influence of Three phase Voltage Imbalance on the Low Voltage Side of Ultra high Voltage Single phase Transformers
results.citation: Tao Tong et al. (2024) Research on the Influence of Three phase Voltage Imbalance on the Low Voltage Side of Ultra high Voltage Single phase Transformers doi:10.1109/AEES63781.2024.10872613
results.venue: 2024 IEEE 5th International Conference on Advanced Electrical and Energy Systems (AEES)
results.abstract: In order to study the effect of the capacitance difference of single-phase ultra-high voltage (UHV) transformer windings on the three-phase voltage imbalance of low-voltage ungrounded systems, an equivalent model of the power and load characteristics of electrical equipment such as transformers, compensation devices, and measurement devices was established by decoupling the high and medium voltage windings of transformers from the grounding capacity; Comparative analysis of the impact of three-phase voltage imbalance in windings at different positions on ungrounded systems. The results indicate that the voltage balance on the low voltage side mainly depends on the capacitance difference between the main transformer and the low voltage winding, as well as the operating mode of the low voltage side continuously variable transmission. This can be improved by investing in large grounded capacitor continuously variable transmissions or replacing them with continuously variable transmissions of different capacities. Simulation and field experiments have verified the accuracy and practicality of this method.

papergraph_input.citation: Tao Tong et al. (2024) Research on the Influence of Three phase Voltage Imbalance on the Low Voltage Side of Ultra high Voltage Single phase Transformers doi:10.1109/AEES63781.2024.10872613
papergraph_input.venue: 2024 IEEE 5th International Conference on Advanced Electrical and Energy Systems (AEES)
papergraph_input.abstract: In order to study the effect of the capacitance difference of single-phase ultra-high voltage (UHV) transformer windings on the three-phase voltage imbalance of low-voltage ungrounded systems, an equivalent model of the power and load characteristics of electrical equipment such as transformers, compensation devices, and measurement devices was established by decoupling the high and medium voltage windings of transformers from the grounding capacity; Comparative analysis of the impact of three-phase voltage imbalance in windings at different positions on ungrounded systems. The results indicate that the voltage balance on the low voltage side mainly depends on the capacitance difference between the main transformer and the low voltage winding, as well as the operating mode of the low voltage side continuously variable transmission. This can be improved by investing in large grounded capacitor continuously variable transmissions or replacing them with continuously variable transmissions of different capacities. Simulation and field experiments have verified the accuracy and practicality of this method.

results.title: Audio Detection and Fault Diagnosis of Ultra-High Voltage Transformers
results.citation: Wu Dong et al. (2024) Audio Detection and Fault Diagnosis of Ultra-High Voltage Transformers doi:10.1109/ICSADL61749.2024.00124
results.venue: 2024 3rd International Conference on Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning (ICSADL)
results.abstract: This research work addresses the critical issue of audio detection and fault diagnosis in Ultra High Voltage (UHV) transformers, essential components of power transmission systems. Various faults such as short-circuits, insulation problems and oil temperature anomalies can have a significant impact on power converters and the entire power grid. This study proposes a novel approach using audio signals for real-time fault detection and diagnosis. The proposed methodology involves the extraction of audio signals from ultra-high voltage transformers, taking into account factors such as peak frequency, frequency offset, and total harmonic distortion (THD). A deep neural network is used for systematic analysis and interpretation of these features to enable accurate fault detection. Moreover, this study presents a thorough verification process using an experimental dataset, demonstrating superior detection accuracy compared to existing models. The dataset includes diverse samples of normal and fault scenarios, and the proposed model consistently achieves accuracy levels between 98% and 100%. The results suggest the effectiveness of the proposed audio-based methodology for enabling reliable fault detection in ultrahigh-voltage transformers.

results.title: Evaluation of Electromagnetic Shielding Performance for the Monitoring Unit Shield of an Ultra-High Voltage Converter Valve
results.citation: Guangxin Jiang et al. (2024) Evaluation of Electromagnetic Shielding Performance for the Monitoring Unit Shield of an Ultra-High Voltage Converter Valve doi:10.1109/HVDC62448.2024.10723012
results.venue: 2024 International Conference on HVDC (HVDC)
results.abstract: UHVDC transmission technology is an important means for the power grid to achieve large-scale power transmission. The performance of its core equipment, the converter valve, is closely related to the operational reliability of the DC transmission project. In order to achieve reliable triggering, protection, and monitoring functions for the converter valve, the circuit board of the online monitoring unit in the valve tower plays a key role. However, due to the circuit board being directly installed near the thyristors inside the converter valve module, it often operates in a harsh environment with high potential, strong electromagnetic fields, and intense heat. If the electromagnetic compatibility performance of the monitoring unit does not meet the requirements, it may lead to issues such as reduced measurement accuracy, high communication error rates, and communication errors. Therefore, this study focuses on the research of electromagnetic protection for the monitoring unit circuit board. First, based on the ± 800kV converter valve structure, a full-circuit simulation model of the converter valve was established. Then, the calculation method of the electromagnetic field during the operation of the converter valve was analyzed. Combining the frequency-domain finite integration method and circuit time-domain calculation, the level of disturbance caused by the switch-off voltage and turn-on current on the PCB board of the TTM were evaluated. Additionally, the shielding performance of the shield cover on the PCB for far-field and near-field disturbances was assessed. The study concludes that the shield cover can effectively protect against the near-field disturbances generated by the valve module, providing theoretical support for the electromagnetic protection design of the online monitoring unit of the converter valve.

results.title: Spare Parts Management Strategy for Seismic Resilience Improvement of Ultra-high Voltage Converter Station Converter Station
results.citation: Zhihang Xue et al. (2024) Spare Parts Management Strategy for Seismic Resilience Improvement of Ultra-high Voltage Converter Station Converter Station doi:10.1109/ACFPE63443.2024.10800757
results.venue: 2024 3rd Asian Conference on Frontiers of Power and Energy (ACFPE)
results.abstract: Improving resilience of power system is an important aspect of smart grid research. Ultra-high voltage (UHV) converter station serves as core node for power grid and appropriate spare parts can significantly shorten post-earthquake repair time of the converter station and therefore enhance its seismic resilience. This article takes spare parts strategy of the UHV converter station under budget constraint as its research object. Firstly, a functional component model of the UHV converter station is established, and post-earthquake transmission function indicator and seismic resilience index R with per-unit characteristics are defined. Subsequently, the two-stage stochastic programming (TSSP) method was employed to construct a two-stage optimization model for the seismic resilience improvement of the UHV converter station. The sample average approximation (SAA) method was used to solve the model. Lastly, the effectiveness of the proposed method was validated using data from the ± 800kV UHV converter station.

results.title: Load-Dependent Performance and Safety Assessment of DS-PST Based Online Ice-Covered Method for 500kV Regional Power Grid Interconnection Line
results.citation: Jun Wu et al. (2026) Load-Dependent Performance and Safety Assessment of DS-PST Based Online Ice-Covered Method for 500kV Regional Power Grid Interconnection Line doi:10.1109/ACPEE69242.2026.11524982
results.venue: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
results.abstract: The ice-covered lines phenomenon on ultra-high voltage transmission lines seriously affects the safe operation and maintenance of the power grid. Traditional ice-melting methods require line outages and suffer from low efficiency. This paper presents a systematic study on the load-dependent performance and safety assessment of online ice-melting for a 500kV regional grid interconnection line based on dual-core symmetrical phase-shifting transformers (DS-PST). A theoretical framework is established, including the DS-PST equivalent impedance model, the load factor λ definition, the natural current distribution under constant power load, and the derivation of critical ice-melting current and maximum allowable current. A simulation model of the 500kV regional grid with five interconnection lines is developed. Three DS-PST installation schemes are evaluated under four load levels: light load (λ=0.3), normal load (λ=0.6), heavy load (λ=0.9), and full load (λ=1.0). Safety assessment confirms that Scheme 2 maintains bus voltages within ±5% of nominal and line currents below the maximum allowable limit across all load levels. Consequently, Scheme 2 strikes the optimal balance between ice-melting efficacy and economic rationality across a wide range of load conditions, making it the recommended implementation strategy.

results.title: Online Ice-Covered Suppression Strategy Research for 500kV Regional Power Grid Interconnection Line Based on Phase Shifting Transformer
results.citation: Jun Wu et al. (2026) Online Ice-Covered Suppression Strategy Research for 500kV Regional Power Grid Interconnection Line Based on Phase Shifting Transformer doi:10.1109/ACPEE69242.2026.11525202
results.venue: 2026 11th Asia Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering (ACPEE)
results.abstract: The ice-covered lines phenomenon on ultra-high voltage transmission lines seriously affects the safe operation and maintenance of the power grid. Traditional ice-melting methods have shortcomings such as the need for offline power outages and low efficiency. This paper presents a online ice-covered suppression strategy research for 500kV regional power grid interconnection line based on Phase Shifting Transformer (PST). Firstly, the power flow regulation principle of PST in parallel lines is established, and the critical ice-melting current and the maximum ice-melting current of the line were derived. Then, a simulation model of the 500kV regional power grid connection line is established to explore the influence of different PST installation schemes on lines prone to icing. Finally, technical and economic analyses of different schemes were conducted. Analysis indicates that Scheme Two, by installing two additional PSTs, enables Line 2 and Line 3 to achieve maximum currents of 2337A and 3347A respectively, thereby effectively mitigating ice-covered hazards. Consequently, Scheme Two strikes the optimal balance between technical efficacy and economic rationality, making it the recommended preferred implementation option.

results.title: Knowledge reasoning in power grid infrastructure projects based on deep multi-view graph convolutional network
results.citation: Jie Hu et al. (2024) Knowledge reasoning in power grid infrastructure projects based on deep multi-view graph convolutional network doi:10.3389/fenrg.2023.1339416
results.venue: Frontiers in Energy Research
results.abstract: With the rapid development of power grid infrastructure, especially the increasing number of ultra-high voltage (UHV) projects, knowledge extracted from historical engineering data is collected and can be potentially used to assist in the review of power transmission and transformation projects. However, conventional knowledge modeling and knowledge reasoning methods cannot meet the current needs of power grid construction. In this paper, considering the more supernumerary and distinctive information brought by multi-view data which could be beneficial for feature representation and knowledge reasoning from the constructed knowledge base, a multi-view graph convolutional network (GCN) based on knowledge graph is proposed to make classification for power grid infrastructure projects. Specifically, several views are constructed based on attribute information of a knowledge graph. In addition, a Haar convolution-based pooling mechanism is employed to capture the structural features represented by a chain of subgraphs. And then an aggregator that combines both attribute and structural information is used to classify UHV projects. Results from both UHV and NCI-1 datasets indicate that our proposed method is more has higher accuracy and generalization ability.

results.title: Quantification of Transient Voltage Rise in UHVDC Sending End Power Grid Under Commutation Failure
results.citation: Haoran Ge et al. (2025) Quantification of Transient Voltage Rise in UHVDC Sending End Power Grid Under Commutation Failure doi:10.3390/electronics14244855
results.venue: Electronics
results.abstract: Ultra-high-voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission serves as a vital method for long-distance transmission of renewable energy in China. Commutation failure represents a common fault type in UHVDC transmission systems, causing the sending-end bus voltage to exhibit a “low-to-high” characteristic. This phenomenon poses a high-voltage disconnection risk for renewable energy units at the sending end. The high-voltage ride-through criteria for renewable energy incorporate both time and voltage peak factors. However, existing research relies solely on the voltage peak metric to assess disconnection risks for renewable units, failing to determine the specific stability level of the voltage. Therefore, this paper considers the cumulative effect of voltage transients over time, constructing a mathematical model of transient voltage during the entire fault process of a UHVDC transmission system at the sending end under commutation failure. Subsequently, a transient voltage rise stability margin metric based on a multi-binary table is proposed to evaluate the system’s transient voltage rise stability margin from both time and voltage peak dimensions. Finally, the accuracy of the proposed mathematical model and evaluation metric is validated using the PSCAD/EMTDC simulation platform. Results indicate that following a commutation failure in a UHVDC system, under the scenario of overvoltage instability alone, a higher short-circuit ratio (SCR) correlates with a lower system rated voltage. This configuration enhances the voltage stability margin of the sending end grid, improves its transient voltage stability, and helps mitigate the risk of renewable energy units disconnecting from the sending end grid.

results.title: Comparing a Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current and Unified Power Flow Control for Short Circuit Level Reduction in Iraqi 400 Kv Grid
results.citation: A. N. Merzah et al. (2024) Comparing a Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current and Unified Power Flow Control for Short Circuit Level Reduction in Iraqi 400 Kv Grid doi:10.51244/ijrsi.2024.1107018
results.venue: International journal of research and scientific innovation
results.abstract: The demand for electricity has increased significantly in recent years; yet, the transmission and distribution networks are unable to meet unrestrained demands because of resource constraint. Losses in power lines exacerbate the unfavorable conditions for maximum power transfer. Recent decades have seen a rise in the use of FACTS and High Voltage Direct Current transmission in power systems due to its many advantages, which include reduced short circuit level (SCL), enhanced voltage profiles, reduced network power losses, and enhanced system reliability and safety. The paper presents a comparing study of the effectiveness of Unified Power Flow Control (UPFC) and Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current (MT-HVDC) transmission in reducing short circuit current and network power losses for the Iraqi Super High Voltage (ISHV) grid. Modeling of Unified Power Flow Control and Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current transmission is carried out using the Power System Simulator for Engineering (PSS/E) version 32 Package Program. The integration of multi-terminal HVDC transmission to the system resulted in a considerable decrease in short circuit levels and network power losses for the majority of buses in the system, as well as a reduction in the loading on the transmission lines in the southern region of Iraqi Super High Voltage grid, As a result, the South’s network performance will improve, hence improving Iraq’s electrical power system. Furthermore, when compared to installing a UPFC, the results showed that multi-terminal HVDC transmission reduces short circuit levels more efficiently.

results.title: Monitoring and Simulation of Curing-Induced Residual Strain in Epoxy Core of Ultra-High-Voltage Bushing
results.citation: Yu Zhang et al. (2026) Monitoring and Simulation of Curing-Induced Residual Strain in Epoxy Core of Ultra-High-Voltage Bushing doi:10.3390/en19112718
results.venue: Energies
results.abstract: The UHV dry-type bushing plays a critical role in power transmission by enabling electrical connection, electrical insulation, and mechanical support, making it a core component for ensuring the safe and stable operation of UHV direct current (DC) transmission projects. Epoxy resin, serving as the fundamental insulating material for the bushing core, undergoes significant residual strain during high-temperature curing due to chemical shrinkage and thermal strain, which directly affects the molding quality and service reliability of the component. This paper investigates the curing process of a large-thickness epoxy material, which is on the same scale as a UHV bushing. An in situ monitoring system combining fiber Bragg grating (FBG) sensors and thermocouples, together with COMSOL Multiphysics simulations, is employed to systematically study the evolution of the temperature field and residual strain throughout the entire curing process, considering the demolding effect. The results show that during the curing stage, the internal temperature distribution is non-uniform, with a maximum temperature difference of 65 °C between the center and the edge. The residual strain is dominated by chemical shrinkage (accounting for 73.25%) and exhibits a pronounced radial gradient. Mold constraint and demolding cause abrupt changes in the strain. The developed thermo-chemo-mechanical coupled model shows good agreement between simulations and experimental measurements. Thermal cycling relaxes the residual stress, achieving a reduction of 3.89–5.77%. This study provides support for process optimization and defect prevention in large-scale epoxy insulation components.

results.title: Study on Power Supply Reliability of Sending End Matching Power Access to Ultra-high Voltage AC and DC Hybrid Power Grid
results.citation: Zheng Bowen et al. (2018) Study on Power Supply Reliability of Sending End Matching Power Access to Ultra-high Voltage AC and DC Hybrid Power Grid doi:10.1109/CICED.2018.8592243
results.venue: China International Conference on Electricity Distribution
results.abstract: With the continuous operation of ultra-high voltage (UHV) AC and DC power transmission and transformation projects in Inner Mongolia autonomous region, Inner Mongolia power grid has become a UHV AC and DC hybrid system. However, the development of AC and DC projects is not balanced, and the characteristics of “strong DC and weak AC” are obvious, It is bound to affect the safe and stable operation of UHV power grids, when sending end matching thermal power units connecting to UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid, the reliability of power supply is difficult to be guaranteed. Taking the sending end regional power grid of Xilinhot to Taizhou (Jiangsu Province) UHV DC transmission project (hereinafter referred to as Xitai DC) as the research object, by using Power System Analysis Software Package simulation software, this paper deeply analyzes the influence of high-power matching power supply on the safe and stable operation of the power grid after connecting to the UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid, and put forward the corresponding prevention and control measures.

papergraph_input.venue: China International Conference on Electricity Distribution
papergraph_input.abstract: With the continuous operation of ultra-high voltage (UHV) AC and DC power transmission and transformation projects in Inner Mongolia autonomous region, Inner Mongolia power grid has become a UHV AC and DC hybrid system. However, the development of AC and DC projects is not balanced, and the characteristics of “strong DC and weak AC” are obvious, It is bound to affect the safe and stable operation of UHV power grids, when sending end matching thermal power units connecting to UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid, the reliability of power supply is difficult to be guaranteed. Taking the sending end regional power grid of Xilinhot to Taizhou (Jiangsu Province) UHV DC transmission project (hereinafter referred to as Xitai DC) as the research object, by using Power System Analysis Software Package simulation software, this paper deeply analyzes the influence of high-power matching power supply on the safe and stable operation of the power grid after connecting to the UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid, and put forward the corresponding prevention and control measures.

results.title: Renewable Energy Base Power Transmission Reliability Evaluation Method Considering Power Balance and Wind Power Output Coefficient
results.citation: Shin-Je Li et al. (2025) Renewable Energy Base Power Transmission Reliability Evaluation Method Considering Power Balance and Wind Power Output Coefficient doi:10.1109/ITOEC63606.2025.10968370
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 8th Information Technology and Mechatronics Engineering Conference (ITOEC)
results.abstract: The application of large-capacity ultra-high voltage direct current (UHVDC) transmission greatly improves the large-scale resource allocation capability. The randomness and intermittency of renewable energy power generation often lead to power deviations of up to millions of kilowatts. This poses great challenges for the electricity consumption within the sending-end system, satisfying large-scale external transmission, and ensuring the reliability of system power supply. Based on the historical data and the wind turbine output coefficient calculated by power grid planners according to work experience, the probability that the output system coefficient calculated by historical data is greater than or less than the wind turbine output coefficient calculated by experience is analyzed. Next, the 95% confidence interval is considered to ensure that the output coefficient corresponding to more than 95% of the data point satisfies the requirements and that the corresponding wind turbine output coefficient is obtained. The change of the system power surplus caused by the empirical value and the newly obtained value of the output coefficient is calculated. Then, the power balance evaluation index is defined to characterize the judgment of power surplus by selecting a new wind turbine output coefficient. Finally, the effectiveness and adaptability of the proposed method are verified by a provincial power grid.

results.title: Study on the Electromagnetic Calculations and Failure Mechanisms of Secondary Equipment in High-Voltage Series Compensation Platforms Based on Field-Circuit Coupling
results.citation: Chenye Zhou et al. (2025) Study on the Electromagnetic Calculations and Failure Mechanisms of Secondary Equipment in High-Voltage Series Compensation Platforms Based on Field-Circuit Coupling doi:10.1109/EEPS68057.2025.11351728
results.venue: 2025 5th IEEE International Conference on Energy Engineering and Power Systems (EEPS)
results.abstract: Ultra-High Voltage (UHV) series compensation devices significantly boost power transmission capacity (by 30 % 50 %) and grid stability by reducing line inductive reactance [1]. The secondary equipment on such platforms, acting as the “nervous center” for primary equipment monitoring, control, and protection [1], [2], is critical to operational safety. However, its compact layout ($\leq 1.5 \mathrm{m}$ from primary equipment) exposes it to severe electromagnetic interference (EMI): primary operations (e.g., disconnector switching, spark gap breakdown) generate Fast Transient Over-voltages (FTOVs, up to 140 kV, tens of nanoseconds rise time [2], [3]) that disrupt secondary functions via conduction, radiation, and magnetic coupling. Current research has gaps: simplified 2D models fail to capture 3D electric field distributions [4]; failure mechanism analysis lacks quantification of overvoltage-shielding-failure correlations [5], [6]; and shielding design relies on experience rather than field-based optimization [3]. With EMI-induced secondary equipment failures accounting for $\mathbf{2 3 \% - 3 5 \%}$ of total faults (causing protection misoperation and million-yuan losses per outage [2], [6]), targeted research is urgent. This paper investigates the failure mechanisms and EMI characteristics of UHV series compensation platform secondary equipment under overvoltage. Using the finite element method, a full 3D electromagnetic-structure coupling model is built for shielded and unshielded scenarios. By applying primary system overvoltage signals and analyzing electric field distributions, the equipment's failure behavior and EMI response are examined. The study provides precise support for anti-interference design of high-voltage series compensation platform secondary equipment.

results.title: Study on the Influence of Large-scale New Energy Connect to the Operation Characteristics of Ultra-high Voltage Sending end Network with Weak Synchronization Support
results.citation: Bowen Zheng et al. (2021) Study on the Influence of Large-scale New Energy Connect to the Operation Characteristics of Ultra-high Voltage Sending end Network with Weak Synchronization Support doi:10.1109/CEEPE51765.2021.9475769
results.venue: 2021 4th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
results.abstract: In recent years, new energy sources such as wind power and photovoltaic have entered a period of rapid development at home and abroad. The unbalanced distribution of energy and load determines the main characteristics of large-scale development and centralized delivery of new energy in China. Therefore, large capacity and long-distance UHV transmission technology has become an important carrier of intensive and efficient development and large-scale allocation and consumption of clean energy. However, due to its characteristics of weak inertia, large output fluctuation and poor reactive power support, new energy seriously affects the safe and stable operation of the power grid, especially for the sending end grid with weak synchronization support, the large-scale access of new energy will have a inestimable impact on the transmission capacity and operation characteristics of UHV, and the reliability of power supply is difficult to be guaranteed. In this paper, PSASP (power system analysis software package) simulation software is used to study the transmission end power grid of Ximeng UHV AC / DC transmission project, the simulation analysis of the system strength, stability level, power transmission capacity and other operation characteristics of the power grid in the region after the planned new energy access is carried out, the impact of large-scale new energy on the safe and stable operation of UHV AC-DC hybrid power grid is analyzed in depth, and the corresponding effective prevention and control measures are proposed.

results.title: Three-dimensional electro-thermal coupling analysis of ultra-high-voltage autotransformer based on MPI-PETSc parallel computing framework
results.citation: E. Tianlong et al. (2025) Three-dimensional electro-thermal coupling analysis of ultra-high-voltage autotransformer based on MPI-PETSc parallel computing framework doi:10.1186/s40807-025-00207-6
results.venue: Sustainable Energy Research
results.abstract: Ultra-high-voltage (UHV) autotransformers are widely employed in long-distance power transmission systems. Their operation involves complex energy conversion and coupling mechanisms, including high-intensity magnetic induction energy and strong induced currents. From the perspective of power systems and automation control, it is essential to construct a comprehensive equivalent control circuit for UHV autotransformers, integrating the analysis of induced current and magnetic flux density into the domain of analog electronics. Numerical analysis has become a core approach for investigating the external thermal physical characteristics of transformer power and various thermal management strategies. In this paper, the Message Passing Interface (MPI) and Portable, Extensible Toolkit for Scientific Computation (PETSc) parallel computing framework is adopted to compute and analyze the electro-thermal coupling in a UHV autotransformer. The dielectric loss of transformer components is thoroughly examined. A linear numerical simulation method for evaluating dielectric loss is assessed through parallel computation and validated via the design of a three-dimensional coupling model for leakage flux and core temperature rise. The dielectric loss calculation is applied to the transformer. Magnetostriction measurements under rated output power and various current and voltage conditions reveal the correlation between the coupled data and the thermal topology. The MPI-PETSc framework significantly enhances the computational efficiency of three-dimensional electro-thermal coupling problems in UHV autotransformers through distributed computing and efficient numerical solving, making it suitable for large-scale, high-precision engineering simulations.

results.title: Coordinated reactive power control for hybrid cascaded high‐voltage direct‐current links in weak AC grids
results.citation: Xiaojie Fan et al. (2024) Coordinated reactive power control for hybrid cascaded high‐voltage direct‐current links in weak AC grids doi:10.1049/gtd2.13143
results.venue: IET Generation, Transmission & Distribution
results.abstract: The hybrid cascaded high‐voltage direct‐current (HC‐HVDC) links are recently introduced for ultra‐high voltage bulky power transmissions. However, as wind power becomes more prevalent, the grid is gradually weakened and will face voltage stability issues. This paper proposes a coordinated reactive power control strategy for HC‐HVDC links to enhance the voltage stability of AC bus. Firstly, the impact of HC‐HVDC on the static voltage stability of the AC bus is studied by the voltage sensitivity coefficient. Then, the limitation of reactive power capability of modular multilevel converter in the HC structure is identified. Based on the analysis results, a coordinated reactive power control strategy based on adaptive voltage droop is proposed. In the strategy, the DC voltage ratio is regulated according to the fluctuation degree of the AC bus voltage to adjust the reactive power of HC‐HVDC, which realized the dynamic voltage support for the AC bus. Finally, electromagnetic simulations in power systems computer aided design (PSCAD)/electromagnetic transients including DC (EMTDC) are performed, and the results validate the theoretical findings and the effectiveness of the control strategy.

results.title: Active Power Coordinated Control of Grid-Forming Renewable Generations and DC Transmission System
results.citation: Zhi Jing et al. (2024) Active Power Coordinated Control of Grid-Forming Renewable Generations and DC Transmission System doi:10.1109/ICPEE64457.2024.10950001
results.venue: 2024 8th International Conference on Power and Energy Engineering (ICPEE)
results.abstract: In recent years, the large-scale wind and photovoltaic power bases have been actively promoted in desert and arid regions, with the construction of ultra-high voltage (UHV) transmission channels. However, faults in the DC transmission channels of these new energy bases can lead to short-term active power fluctuations, potentially causing instability in the angle and frequency deviations in the sending-end power system. This paper first establishes a model of a wind-solar power base under ideal conditions, where the wind power system employs a network-forming control strategy to enhance grid connection stability, while the photovoltaic system continues to use the traditional grid-following control. Through theoretical analysis of the spatiotemporal distribution characteristics of frequency in the new energy power system, the paper explores the factors influencing these distribution characteristics, discusses the necessity of studying these characteristics, and develops a coordinated energy flow control strategy for the wind-solar power base. Simulation results verify the accuracy of the proposed frequency indices and the effectiveness of the control strategy, summarizing the frequency variation patterns considering spatiotemporal distribution effects.

results.title: Selection and application of new conductors in the capacity expansion and renovation of high-voltage lines
results.citation: Honglei Yang et al. (2025) Selection and application of new conductors in the capacity expansion and renovation of high-voltage lines doi:10.1117/12.3089149
results.venue: The International Conference on Wireless, Optical Communication and Information Engineering
results.abstract: Traditional conductors generally face the bottlenecks of insufficient current-carrying capacity and poor thermal stability under high-load and high-temperature operating conditions, which have been difficult to meet the actual needs of current power grid capacity expansion and transformation. This paper focuses on the capacity expansion goal of high-voltage lines, constructs a performance index system covering five major dimensions including mechanical, electrical, thermal stability, environmental adaptability and economy, objectively assigns weights by using the entropy weight method, and combines the fuzzy comprehensive evaluation model to realize the design of a new conductor selection scheme. In a typical 220 kV project case, the JNRLH60/LB20A-300/70 type aluminum-clad steel core heat-resistant aluminum alloy conductor was selected to replace the traditional LGJ-300/40 conductor. As verified by field monitoring, the current-carrying capacity increased by 46.7% and the line loss decreased by 15.7%, significantly improving the transmission capacity and operational efficiency. The research results provide a systematic and data-driven technical path for the selection of conductors in high-voltage lines.

results.title: Comparative assessment of fault detection methods in transmission line during power swing: analyzing single and hybrid networks for the southern Kerala grid
results.citation: S. K et al. (2024) Comparative assessment of fault detection methods in transmission line during power swing: analyzing single and hybrid networks for the southern Kerala grid doi:10.1088/2631-8695/ad3895
results.venue: Engineering Research Express
results.abstract: The blocking function of power oscillation is a highly effective feature found in distance relays. Its purpose is to prevent unintentional tripping of transmission lines. It is crucial to accurately differentiate between faults and power oscillations to minimise the occurrence of widespread power outages and monetary losses. Therefore, the detection and classification of faults arising from power swings are critical challenges in ensuring the smooth operation and overall health of transmission lines, particularly in extra-high-voltage (EHV) and ultra-high-voltage (UHV) power systems. Modern methods rely on machine learning methods which are in their infancy. The paper proposes to identify various fault conditions in transmission lines during power swing by using a hybrid Neural Network architecture. In this research, different designs such as 2-D convolution neural network (2D-CNN), 1-D convolution neural network (1D-CNN), long short-term memory (LSTM), and CNN-LSTM hybrid networks are investigated to understand the effect of various deep learning approaches. Along with the southern Kerala grid, the standard 9-bus system is selected as a test case to examine various fault circumstances of the system during power swing. Multiple tests were conducted to determine the optimal deep learning architecture, including model parameters and configurations, for accurate fault detection. For the 9 bus system, the 1D CNN network performs better with an accuracy of 98.70%, and for the Kerala grid, both networks are competitive, but the CNN-LSTM Hybrid method slightly outperforms the 1D CNN with an accuracy of 92.40%.

results.title: N‐Vinylcarbazole Group Grafted Modified Silicone Rubber for Dielectric Performance Improvement of High‐Voltage Insulation
results.citation: Jie Mao et al. (2025) N‐Vinylcarbazole Group Grafted Modified Silicone Rubber for Dielectric Performance Improvement of High‐Voltage Insulation doi:10.1002/app.57757
results.venue: Journal of Applied Polymer Science
results.abstract: Silicone rubber serves as a critical insulating material in electrical engineering due to its outstanding antifouling characteristics, mechanical robustness, and electrical performance. Nevertheless, its insulating properties require further enhancement to meet the demanding requirements of ultra‐high voltage (UHV) applications, particularly for long‐distance, high‐capacity power transmission. In this work, we developed an improved insulating silicone rubber by grafting p–p conjugated N‐vinyl carbazole (VK) groups—known for their space charge trapping capability—onto thiol‐silicone oil, followed by cross‐linking with end‐methyl branched vinyl silicone oil. The modified material demonstrated optimal insulating performance at a VK group concentration of 0.5 wt%, achieving a 23.19% increase in breakdown field strength compared to unmodified silicone rubber films. This study provides a viable approach for enhancing the dielectric strength of polymer‐based silicone rubber materials.

results.title: Geomagnetically Induced Current Calculation of High Voltage Power System With Long Transmission Lines Using Kriging Method
results.citation: Yu-hao Chen et al. (2022) Geomagnetically Induced Current Calculation of High Voltage Power System With Long Transmission Lines Using Kriging Method doi:10.1109/TPWRD.2021.3068216
results.venue: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
results.abstract: Calculation of geomagnetically induced current (GIC) flowing through power system during the geomagnetic storm has attracted more attention recently. However, for high voltage power systems with transmission lines over hundreds or even thousands of kilometers, the earth model and geomagnetical field generally vary significantly. So, it is essential to take them into consideration using limited earth survey sites and geomagnetic observatories. To address this problem, a Kriging method is introduced in this paper to make earth model and geomagnetical field interpolations. It has the characteristic of spatial autocorrelation by considering not only the distances between predicted points and training points but also the distances between training points themselves. Finally, a case study of the Central China 1000 kV ultra-high voltage (UHV) grid is carried out to illustrate the applicability and effectiveness of the proposed method.

papergraph_input.citation: Yu-hao Chen et al. (2022) Geomagnetically Induced Current Calculation of High Voltage Power System With Long Transmission Lines Using Kriging Method doi:10.1109/TPWRD.2021.3068216

results.title: Flexible Power Reciprocity Control of Hybrid Cascaded UHVDC Transmission System in Jiangsu Power Grid
results.citation: Zhiwei Wang et al. (2024) Flexible Power Reciprocity Control of Hybrid Cascaded UHVDC Transmission System in Jiangsu Power Grid doi:10.1109/pesgm51994.2024.10688679
results.venue: IEEE Power & Energy Society General Meeting
results.abstract: Ultra High-Voltage Direct Current (UHVDC) transmission system provides an effective solution for large-scale inter-area power transfer from both economic and security perspectives. By comparing the conventional HVDC and flexible HVDC technologies, this paper first presents a novel hybrid cascaded UHVDC scheme for the Baihetan-Jiangsu UHVDC (JSDC) project where the sending end uses LCC-type converters, and the receiving end adopts one LCC converter and three VSC converters to enhance the safety, stability, and economic operation of the bulk power systems. A power reciprocity control strategy is proposed to flexibly allocate power transfers among the three VSC converters for mitigating the severe power shortage issue in South of Jiangsu province. Such a flexible control scheme was implemented in practice, with various advantages explained via case studies. The effectiveness of the proposed power reciprocity control is verified through the actual operation data from April to July 2023 in Jiangsu Province, China, which, for the first time, reached more than 1GW flexible power dispatch among VSC converter stations in the real world.

papergraph_input.title: Flexible Power Reciprocity Control of Hybrid Cascaded UHVDC Transmission System in Jiangsu Power Grid
papergraph_input.citation: Zhiwei Wang et al. (2024) Flexible Power Reciprocity Control of Hybrid Cascaded UHVDC Transmission System in Jiangsu Power Grid doi:10.1109/pesgm51994.2024.10688679
papergraph_input.venue: IEEE Power & Energy Society General Meeting
papergraph_input.abstract: Ultra High-Voltage Direct Current (UHVDC) transmission system provides an effective solution for large-scale inter-area power transfer from both economic and security perspectives. By comparing the conventional HVDC and flexible HVDC technologies, this paper first presents a novel hybrid cascaded UHVDC scheme for the Baihetan-Jiangsu UHVDC (JSDC) project where the sending end uses LCC-type converters, and the receiving end adopts one LCC converter and three VSC converters to enhance the safety, stability, and economic operation of the bulk power systems. A power reciprocity control strategy is proposed to flexibly allocate power transfers among the three VSC converters for mitigating the severe power shortage issue in South of Jiangsu province. Such a flexible control scheme was implemented in practice, with various advantages explained via case studies. The effectiveness of the proposed power reciprocity control is verified through the actual operation data from April to July 2023 in Jiangsu Province, China, which, for the first time, reached more than 1GW flexible power dispatch among VSC converter stations in the real world.

results.title: Research on anomaly identification method for power grid transmission channels based on spatial data
results.citation: Sijia Zheng et al. (2024) Research on anomaly identification method for power grid transmission channels based on spatial data doi:10.1117/12.3052760
results.venue: Other Conferences
results.abstract: Ultra high voltage power lines transmit large-scale electricity to economically developed areas and are currently an important carrier for long-distance power transmission. High voltage overhead lines are one of the main modes of power transmission. Due to the influence of natural environment and human activities, the transmission channel space faces various risks, and it is necessary to promptly investigate and alert potential risks. In response to this issue, this paper proposes a method for identifying anomalies in power grid transmission channels based on spatial data. Real time monitoring data of transmission channels is obtained through acquisition devices such as cameras and locators. Then, the image, video, geographic information and other data are associated and mapped, and a data model of the transmission channel is constructed with the device as the description core. Under extreme weather conditions such as strong wind, rainstorm and snowstorm, the power transmission channel is disturbed by floating objects and light objects in the nature, causing leakage, short circuit and other problems. Based on the established data model, simulate the movement trajectory of various floating objects that may occur, and then simulate and calculate their various impacts on the safety of transmission channels. Digitally deduce the scope of impact and the secondary accidents caused and determine the impact range of transmission channel risks caused by this event on power supply capacity failures.

results.title: The Overvoltage of Hybrid Transmission Line of Long Distance Ultra-high Voltage Cable and Overhead Line
results.citation: Guoqing Yang et al. (2020) The Overvoltage of Hybrid Transmission Line of Long Distance Ultra-high Voltage Cable and Overhead Line doi:10.1109/AEEES48850.2020.9121503
results.venue: 2020 Asia Energy and Electrical Engineering Symposium (AEEES)
results.abstract: With the rapid development of urban construction, the application of long-distance ultra-high voltage cable becomes an inevitable trend. However, due to larger equivalent capacitance value per unit length of the cable line, larger charging power of the line and the obvious capacitive effect, the overvoltage generated after connecting to the system is more prominent and complex than the overhead line. In this paper, the operating overvoltage and lightning overvoltage of a hybrid line of 330kV high-voltage AC cable and overhead line in a certain region is studied by using ATP/EMTP electromagnetic transient simulation software. Besides, the simulation calculation model of overvoltage is established. The overvoltage level is calculated through the amplitude of waveform, and the overvoltage characteristics and its influencing factors are analyzed. On the basis, the corresponding suppression and lightning protection measures are proposed. As the research results show, the closing no-load line can produce the highest closing overvoltage of about 2.06p.u at the cable side. The overvoltage at the cable side can be effectively dropped to 1.53p.u after installing the closing resistor at the circuit breaker side and adjusting the closing phase Angle. In addition, when the lightning current directly strikes the overhead line, the lightning overvoltage of the cable side reaches 1028kV, and the overvoltage of the overhead line reaches the maximum of about 1320kV. Installing lightning arrester in the combination place of overhead line-cable and in the middle section of overhead line can effectively reduces the lightning overvoltage at the cable side and at the overhead line to 790kV and 1096kV, respectively. The research results offer a necessary basis for the insulation coordination in the engineering application of hybrid line of 330kV cable and overhead line.

results.title: System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission
results.citation: Yaru Zhang et al. (2018) System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission doi:10.1063/1.5013177
results.venue: Journal of Renewable and Sustainable Energy
results.abstract: Energy transportation is an important issue for China's electricity system due to the unbalanced distribution of energy sources and electrical demands. The traditional method of energy transportation (e.g., coal transportation) is challenging due to both the long distance of transportation and the large amount of energy that it requires. In recent years, China has been developing ultra-high voltage (UHV) as an alternative way of transmitting energy over a long distance, and many UHV lines are being planned for construction during the 13th Five-Year Plan (FYP) period. This paper develops a system optimization model for exploring the optimal pathway for long-distance energy transportation in China during the 13th FYP period from the perspective of the minimum cost of an energy transportation system. The results show that (a) although UHV transmission will be increasingly adopted in the 13th FYP period, coal transportation will still play an important role; (b) eight UHV lines are suggested to be built and put into use as a priority in the 13th FYP period; (c) the high technology learning effect, electricity demand, and investment costs of power plants would promote the adoption of UHV. Based on these results, some policy suggestions are provided.Energy transportation is an important issue for China's electricity system due to the unbalanced distribution of energy sources and electrical demands. results.title: Influence of voltage waveform on DC aging characteristics of ZnO varistor in ultra-high voltage power transmission
results.citation: Rong Shi et al. (2019) Influence of voltage waveform on DC aging characteristics of ZnO varistor in ultra-high voltage power transmission doi:10.1109/ICIEA.2019.8834011
results.venue: 2019 14th IEEE Conference on Industrial Electronics and Applications (ICIEA)
results.abstract: Due to its excellent nonlinear characteristics, ZnO resistors have been widely used in ultra-high voltage power transmission. The performance of ZnO resistors have an effect on the safe and stable operation of power system, thus the DC aging research of ZnO resistors is urgently needed. In order to study the DC aging characteristics of ZnO varistor, the aging test of ZnO varistor was carried out under different voltage waveforms, and the aging characteristics of ZnO under different voltage waveforms were analyzed. PEA space charge testing technology, SEM scanning electron microscope and EDS testing technology were performed on the samples before and after aging. The results show that the electrical properties of the ZnO varistor are significantly reduced after aging, and the aging of the sample under the DC bus voltage is particularly serious. The results of EDS test show that Zn ions and Bi ions have a translocation and accumulation in the polarization direction during the aging process. The unsymmetrical distribution of space charge further illustrates that DC aging has a single directional charge accumulation and migration. Under the action of DC voltage, the electrical performance of ZnO resistance chip is aging.

results.title: Research on the vibration detection technology of ultra high voltage oil immersed shunt reactor based on voiceprint-infrared fusion
results.citation: Z. Gu et al. (2023) Research on the vibration detection technology of ultra high voltage oil immersed shunt reactor based on voiceprint-infrared fusion doi:10.1117/12.2681341
results.venue: Conference on Image, Signal Processing, and Pattern Recognition
results.abstract: Oil immersed shunt reactor is one of the most important reactive power compensation equipment in Ultra high voltage (UHV) long-distance power transmission and transformation system. When the oil immersed shunt reactor has potential defects, the vibration signal during operation will be different from that under normal condition, which is shown as different defect signals such as noise and abnormal temperature. Compared with traditional vibration sensor detection, acoustic detection and infrared detection realize non-contact detection, which is safer and does not require complex vibration sensor layout. If the two are combined, the detection efficiency can be greatly improved.In this paper, the acoustic characteristics and thermal distribution characteristics of oil immersed shunt reactors under different vibration modes are studied. Based on the beam forming sound source space positioning calculation method, after the integration of voiceprint and infrared image is completed, the acoustic imaging and infrared detection technology of reactor vibration is proposed to realize the simultaneous monitoring of reactor sound temperature state information, and improve the accuracy and rationality of defect identification. In addition, the design scheme of a prototype for on-site acoustic infrared detection of reactor vibration is proposed. The related results of this paper can provide technical support for the application of UHV oil immersed shunt reactor state assessment and diagnosis, and improve the level of equipment operation and maintenance.
